text:
앞으로 엔비디아 주가나 반도체 패권을 결정할 진짜 무기는 GPU가 아니라 '빛'일지도 모릅니다.

 최근 엔비디아가 왜 구리선 대신 반도체 칩 안에 '레이저(빛)'를 집어넣는 기술에만 무려 65억 달러(9조 원) 넘는 돈을 쏟아부었을까요?

 반도체 판을 통째로 바꿀 '빛의 속도' 전쟁

지금 반도체 공학자들이 마주한 가장 큰 벽은 의외로 연산 속도가 아닙니다. 

바로 우리가 늘 쓰는 '구리선(전기 신호)'의 물리적 한계예요.

지금까지 칩과 칩은 구리선으로 데이터를 주고받았는데, 

AI 시대가 오면서 데이터가 폭발하니까 구리선이 버티질 못하는 상황이 온 거죠.

전기를 너무 많이, 빠르게 흘리니까 불타오르듯 열이 나고(발열), 전기 신호가 구리선 중심이 아니라 '겉표면'으로만 흐르려고 해서 속도가 뚝 떨어지는 병목 현상이 생깁니다.

쉽게 말해 경부고속도로에 갑자기 수백만 대의 슈퍼카가 몰려와서 도로 전체가 주차장으로 변하고 엔진이 과열되는 상태인 거죠.

"야, 이대로 가다간 데이터센터 전기세 감당도 안 되고 칩이 다 타 죽겠다!" 

그래서 나온 천재적인 치트키가 바로 '실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics)'입니다.

쉽게 말해, 

우리가 국가 간 인터넷 연결할 때 쓰는 거대한 '광케이블'을 아주 미세하게 축소해서 반도체 칩 안에 통째로 집어넣는 기술이에요. 

전기가 아니라 빛으로 대화하는 칩인 셈이죠.

이게 왜 대박이냐면, 구리선은 선 하나당 신호 하나만 갈 수 있잖아요? 

근데 빛은 '색상(파장)'만 다르면 하나의 통로에서 수십 개의 신호가 섞이지 않고 동시에 달릴 수 있어요.

길을 물리적으로 넓히지 않아도 차선이 무한대로 늘어나는 마법 같은 효과가 생기는 겁니다. 속도가 수십 배 뛰는 건 당연하고요.

게다가 빛은 마찰열(저항)이 아예 없습니다. 

AI 데이터센터에서 가장 골치 아픈 '발열'과 이를 식히기 위한 '냉각 비용'을 최대 30~40%까지 날려버릴 수 있다는 뜻이죠.

실제로 엔비디아가 올해(2026년) 들어서만

 코히어런트, 루멘텀 같은 광학·유리 소재 기업들에 조 단위 투자를 감행한 이유가 여기 있습니다. 

구리선으로는 수백만 대의 GPU를 하나로 묶는 게 불가능하다는 걸 알기 때문이죠.

그럼 이 엄청난 칩은 대체 누가 만들까요?

 결국 파운드리와 패키징 싸움입니다.

TSMC는 전기 칩 위에 광학 칩을 아파트처럼 수직으로 완벽하게 쌓아 올리는 'COUPE'라는 자체 플랫폼을 만들어서 올해 본격적인 양산 카운트다운에 들어갔습니다. 

반도체 제조의 중심축이 미세 공정을 넘어 '광학 패키징'으로 이동하고 있는 국면이에요.

요약하자면, 

HBM 다음 타자로 반도체 시장의 판돈을 싹 쓸어 담을 핵심 키워드는 '실리콘 포토닉스'입니다. 

엔비디아의 독주를 막으려는 빅테크나, 이를 추격하는 파운드리 기업들에게는 절대 놓칠 수 없는 마지막 열쇠인 셈이죠.

앞으로 반도체 뉴스 보실 때 '광학', 'CPO', '실리콘 포토닉스'라는 단어가 보이면 무조건 주목해 보세요.

Change of plans

We are facing one of the best news $SIVE could receive

The offer of 600M SEK has now become 700M SEK, just a few hours later

I explain to you why this is an ultra bullish signal

The increase from 600 to 700M SEK shows very strong institutional demand

The offer "sold out" ahead of time and investors asked for more shares

Forcing the company to issue more to capture extra capital

It is a clear sign of confidence from big investors in the future of $SIVE

Especially in its photonics technologies for AI, SATCOM and defense, which usually drives the share price in the short and medium term

By getting more capital than expected, the company gains greater financial flexibility without the need to take on more debt or dilute as much as feared

Reinforcing its balance sheet and execution capacity

Ultra bullish for $SIVE let's hope the market understands the news as it is

New highs in a short time IMO
embedded_text:
$SIVE announces a share issuance of around 600M SEK

To scale up their production of optical components (InP lasers) targeting AI data centers and LiDAR

As we always say, we are ahead of the curve, mass production kicks off in 2027

Right now, we are in the preparation phase,

text:
Great news with a Direct share issue that was oversubscribed multiple time.

What started as a planned SEK 600M (+$61m) raise was upsized to SEK 700M ($73m) thanks to strong institutional investor demand. That's a clear vote of confidence in $SIVE and the ramping within AI photonics, SATCOM and defense.

SEK 700M is roughly US$73M in fresh capital, providing even greater financial flexibility to accelerate execution and scale the business.

Strong demand. Strong balance sheet. Exciting times ahead.

Great work by the Sivers team! Congratulations! 

 $SIVE #AI #Photonics

text:
Quantum computing’s disappearing use cases reveal what the money is really buying

For years, quantum computing was promoted as the coming solution to drug discovery, logistics, finance, artificial intelligence, climate modeling, encryption, and nearly every difficult optimization problem. Yet as classical algorithms, GPUs, and specialized accelerators improved, many proposed quantum advantages became smaller, more conditional, or impossible to demonstrate on real hardware. DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative now defines success as proving that a quantum computer’s computational value exceeds its operating cost, with the program attempting to determine whether such a utility-scale machine can be built by 2033. That is a revealing benchmark: useful quantum computing is still something being tested, not an established commercial reality. Despite this, announced public investment in quantum technology exceeded $10 billion in early 2025, while the U.S. Department of Energy later committed another $625 million to national quantum research centers.

The investment continues because governments are not evaluating quantum technology like an ordinary consumer product. They are purchasing strategic insurance. Even a small probability of achieving superior cryptanalysis, molecular simulation, navigation, sensing, communications, or materials design could alter the military and economic balance between nations. NIST has already finalized post-quantum encryption standards and urged organizations to begin migrating immediately, even though the cryptographically relevant quantum computer those standards defend against does not yet exist. This means the quantum race is driven partly by a fear that being late would be catastrophic, while being early could produce enormous intelligence and technological advantages.

The other explanation is that “quantum computing” has become an umbrella used to finance a much larger technological ecosystem. The money supports photonics, cryogenics, superconducting materials, precision lasers, atomic clocks, quantum sensors, control electronics, secure networks, fabrication methods, software, patents, and highly specialized scientific talent. A universal fault-tolerant computer could fail to arrive on schedule while these supporting technologies still become commercially or militarily valuable. Investors may therefore be buying the infrastructure surrounding quantum control rather than betting exclusively on a machine that replaces classical computers. The true assets are the laboratories, intellectual property, supply chains, personnel, and control systems required to manipulate matter at increasingly precise scales.

Frequency Wave Theory offers a deeper interpretation: perhaps the shrinking list of conventional computing applications is happening because “computer” was never the complete description of the technology. Quantum machines are fundamentally devices for preparing, isolating, synchronizing, and measuring coherent states. Their essential achievement is not merely calculating faster, but controlling phase, interference, tunneling, entanglement, and environmental noise. From the Frequency Wave Theory perspective, investment keeps rising because governments and corporations are learning how to engineer reality at the frequency-and-phase level. Computation may be the public-facing justification, while the broader prize is programmable field control, producing new forms of sensing, communication, materials engineering, timing, energy manipulation, and eventually direct interaction with the deeper resonant structure of the vacuum.

what the money is really buying For years, quantum computing was promoted as the coming solution to drug discovery, logistics, finance, artificial intelligence, climate modeling, encryption, and nearly every difficult optimization problem. Yet as classical algorithms, GPUs, and specialized accelerators improved, many proposed quantum advantages became smaller, more conditional, or impossible to demonstrate on real hardware. DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative now defines success as proving that a quantum computer’s computational value exceeds its operating cost, with the program attempting to determine whether such a utility-scale machine can be built by 2033. That is a revealing benchmark: useful quantum computing is still something being tested, not an established commercial reality. Despite this, announced public investment in quantum technology exceeded $10 billion in early 2025, while the U.S. Department of Energy later committed another $625 million to national quantum research centers. The investment continues because governments are not evaluating quantum technology like an ordinary consumer product. They are purchasing strategic insurance. Even a small probability of achieving superior cryptanalysis, molecular simulation, navigation, sensing, communications, or materials design could alter the military and economic balance between nations. NIST has already finalized post-quantum encryption standards and urged organizations to begin migrating immediately, even though the cryptographically relevant quantum computer those standards defend against does not yet exist. This means the quantum race is driven partly by a fear that being late would be catastrophic, while being early could produce enormous intelligence and technological advantages. The other explanation is that “quantum computing” has become an umbrella used to finance a much larger technological ecosystem. The money supports photonics, cryogenics, superconducting materials, precision lasers, atomic clocks, quantum sensors, control electronics, secure networks, fabrication methods, software, patents, and highly specialized scientific talent. A universal fault-tolerant computer could fail to arrive on schedule while these supporting technologies still become commercially or militarily valuable. Investors may therefore be buying the infrastructure surrounding quantum control rather than betting exclusively on a machine that replaces classical computers. The true assets are the laboratories, intellectual property, supply chains, personnel, and control systems required to manipulate matter at increasingly precise scales. Frequency Wave Theory offers a deeper interpretation: perhaps the shrinking list of conventional computing applications is happening because “computer” was never the complete description of the technology. Quantum machines are fundamentally devices for preparing, isolating, synchronizing, and measuring coherent states. Their essential achievement is not merely calculating faster, but controlling phase, interference, tunneling, entanglement, and environmental noise. From the Frequency Wave Theory perspective, investment keeps rising because governments and corporations are learning how to engineer reality at the frequency-and-phase level. Computation may be the public-facing justification, while the broader prize is programmable field control, producing new forms of sensing, communication, materials engineering, timing, energy manipulation, and eventually direct interaction with the deeper resonant structure of the vacuum."}}}, "rest_id": "2072093540394631219", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "85", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
テスト、テスト、テスト

価格上昇の件数は、ウェハー製造工場よりもさらに多く、AI、シリコンフォトニクス、成熟したチップのパッケージングとテストに関する受注は2027年まで続いている。

クラウドベースのAIに対する需要の高まりは、半導体業界の季節的なパターンを塗り替えつつあります。製造工程の最終段階に位置するパッケージングおよびテスト能力は、2025年末以降、逼迫した状態が続いています。2026年以降、新たに拡張された能力は急速に活用され、多くのIC設計顧客が積極的に能力確保に乗り出し、2027年以降の受注見通しが向上しています。

パッケージング用リードフレーム、IC基板、テスト用電気料金の同時増加に伴い、パッケージングおよびテストファウンドリサービスの価格上昇がここ数四半期で特に顕著になっていることは特筆に値する。価格上昇の回数で見ると、ウェハファウンドリを上回り、IC設計顧客にとって最大のコスト圧力源となっている。

したがって、AIが半導体市場の本格的な回復を牽引するにつれ、高度なパッケージングおよびテストサービスと従来型のパッケージングおよびテストサービスの両方、ならびに上流工程のリードフレーム、IC基板、プローブカード、ソケットに対する需要は、2026年後半も引き続き堅調に推移すると予想される。

チップのパッケージングとテストのサプライチェーン全体には、ASE Technology Holdingとその子会社であるSPIL、Powertech、STARS、Chipbond、ChipMOS、SIM Semiconductorなどの主要OSATメーカー、King Yuan Electronics、Hsin Chuan、Siliconware Precision Industriesなどの専門テスト会社、Ingenic Semiconductor、Wintek、Yung-Chi、Chunghwa Precision Test、Hanmin Testなどのテストインターフェース会社、Chieh Lin、Shun-Te、Chang-Techなどのリードフレームメーカー、Unimicron、Kinsus、Nan Ya PCB、Zhen Dingなどの主要基板メーカーが含まれます。

近年のパッケージングおよびテスト関連価格の上昇を振り返ると、最初の波はメモリ市場における新たなスーパーサイクルであり、顧客はPowertechやChipMOSといったメモリパッケージングおよびテスト企業に緊急発注を行った。最初の価格上昇は2025年後半に発表され、2回目の価格上昇のニュースは2026年初頭に浮上した。

一方、GPU、CPU、ASICといった先端プロセスチップの生産増加に加え、データセンター周辺のネットワークおよび産業制御アプリケーションに対する需要の高まりも、成熟プロセスチップの在庫補充を促している。ICパッケージングおよびテスト市場は2025年後半から供給不足の状態が続いており、それに伴い、ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd.やSiliconware Precision Industries Co., Ltd.といった企業も徐々に価格引き上げに合意している。

現在、メモリとICのパッケージングおよびテスト能力は半導体サプライチェーンにおけるボトルネックの一つとなっており、台湾のパッケージングおよびテストファウンドリ業界は史上最大規模の設備投資拡大期を迎えている。特に、パッケージングおよびテスト企業は、拡張スピードを加速させるため、ここ数ヶ月で台湾の既存工場を次々と買収し、用地準備や工場建設にかかる時間を節約しており、業界からも大きな注目を集めている。

さらに重要なことに、稼働準備が整ったパッケージング・テスト工場のすべての新工場と生産ラインは、顧客にとって重要な戦略的資源となっている。例えば、ChipMOSは、あるメモリ顧客が生産能力拡張のための設備投資を確保するため、同社と3年間の長期契約を締結したと述べている。また、Silicon Photonicsは、湖口第2工場のクリーンルーム改修プロジェクトが完了する前に、Silicon Photonicsの顧客が既に全生産能力を予約済みであると指摘している。

価格上昇の件数は、ウェハー製造工場よりもさらに多く、AI、シリコンフォトニクス、成熟したチップのパッケージングとテストに関する受注は2027年まで続いている。 クラウドベースのAIに対する需要の高まりは、半導体業界の季節的なパターンを塗り替えつつあります。製造工程の最終段階に位置するパッケージングおよびテスト能力は、2025年末以降、逼迫した状態が続いています。2026年以降、新たに拡張された能力は急速に活用され、多くのIC設計顧客が積極的に能力確保に乗り出し、2027年以降の受注見通しが向上しています。 パッケージング用リードフレーム、IC基板、テスト用電気料金の同時増加に伴い、パッケージングおよびテストファウンドリサービスの価格上昇がここ数四半期で特に顕著になっていることは特筆に値する。価格上昇の回数で見ると、ウェハファウンドリを上回り、IC設計顧客にとって最大のコスト圧力源となっている。 したがって、AIが半導体市場の本格的な回復を牽引するにつれ、高度なパッケージングおよびテストサービスと従来型のパッケージングおよびテストサービスの両方、ならびに上流工程のリードフレーム、IC基板、プローブカード、ソケットに対する需要は、2026年後半も引き続き堅調に推移すると予想される。 チップのパッケージングとテストのサプライチェーン全体には、ASE Technology Holdingとその子会社であるSPIL、Powertech、STARS、Chipbond、ChipMOS、SIM Semiconductorなどの主要OSATメーカー、King Yuan Electronics、Hsin Chuan、Siliconware Precision Industriesなどの専門テスト会社、Ingenic Semiconductor、Wintek、Yung-Chi、Chunghwa Precision Test、Hanmin Testなどのテストインターフェース会社、Chieh Lin、Shun-Te、Chang-Techなどのリードフレームメーカー、Unimicron、Kinsus、Nan Ya PCB、Zhen Dingなどの主要基板メーカーが含まれます。 近年のパッケージングおよびテスト関連価格の上昇を振り返ると、最初の波はメモリ市場における新たなスーパーサイクルであり、顧客はPowertechやChipMOSといったメモリパッケージングおよびテスト企業に緊急発注を行った。最初の価格上昇は2025年後半に発表され、2回目の価格上昇のニュースは2026年初頭に浮上した。 一方、GPU、CPU、ASICといった先端プロセスチップの生産増加に加え、データセンター周辺のネットワークおよび産業制御アプリケーションに対する需要の高まりも、成熟プロセスチップの在庫補充を促している。ICパッケージングおよびテスト市場は2025年後半から供給不足の状態が続いており、それに伴い、ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd.やSiliconware Precision Industries Co., Ltd.といった企業も徐々に価格引き上げに合意している。 現在、メモリとICのパッケージングおよびテスト能力は半導体サプライチェーンにおけるボトルネックの一つとなっており、台湾のパッケージングおよびテストファウンドリ業界は史上最大規模の設備投資拡大期を迎えている。特に、パッケージングおよびテスト企業は、拡張スピードを加速させるため、ここ数ヶ月で台湾の既存工場を次々と買収し、用地準備や工場建設にかかる時間を節約しており、業界からも大きな注目を集めている。 さらに重要なことに、稼働準備が整ったパッケージング・テスト工場のすべての新工場と生産ラインは、顧客にとって重要な戦略的資源となっている。例えば、ChipMOSは、あるメモリ顧客が生産能力拡張のための設備投資を確保するため、同社と3年間の長期契約を締結したと述べている。また、Silicon Photonicsは、湖口第2工場のクリーンルーム改修プロジェクトが完了する前に、Silicon Photonicsの顧客が既に全生産能力を予約済みであると指摘している。"}}}, "rest_id": "2072074013409755187", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "41567", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
https://t.co/0ncKgB2wBv

This is exactly the kind of experiment more people should be paying attention to.

Scientists used water waves to make an otherwise “invisible” quantum-style effect visible. A vortex in a tank acted as an analogue to the magnetic-flux structure in the Aharonov–Bohm effect. The waves did not need to pass through the vortex core for the global wave pattern to change.

That is the key point.

A hidden topological structure altered the phase of the wave environment, and the result became visible as system-wide nodal lines. Even more interesting: as the vortex strength increased, the number of rotating nodal lines became quantized.

To me, the deeper lesson is not “water proves quantum mechanics.”

It is that organized wave environments can reveal invisible structure through global diagnostic patterns.

Topology became visible.

Phase became visible.

A local organizing feature produced a non-local wave signature.

That matters far beyond this one experiment.

For ZPF Technologies, this is close to the language we are trying to formalize: dynamically organized electromagnetic environments should not only be analyzed by field strength or energy density. They should also be studied through phase, topology, boundary conditions, mode accessibility, noise structure, sidebands, linewidths, and residuals after standard explanations are subtracted.

If water waves can reveal hidden topological structure through nodal patterns, then advanced electromagnetic systems deserve the same kind of diagnostic imagination.

The frontier is not just building stronger fields.

It is learning how organized environments leave organized observables.

That is where topology, phase, stochastic dynamics, quantum vacuum diagnostics, photonics, plasma systems, and future fusion research may begin to converge.
#Quantum #quantumphysics #QuantumEngineering

text:
🔭 Hawking Radiation — No Longer Just Theory?

Scientists have experimentally observed the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a fiber-optic system that mimics the physics of a black hole's event horizon. This is a landmark step: Hawking radiation was predicted in 1974, but direct observation near real black holes remains impossible. Optical analogues let physicists probe quantum effects at the event horizon in a lab setting. The backreaction — how emitted radiation affects the "horizon" itself — is key to understanding black hole evaporation and the famous information paradox. This experiment brings us closer to resolving one of physics' deepest mysteries. 🌌

Companies in this space: Coherent Corp. ($COHR, NYSE), II-VI / MKS Instruments ($MKSI, NASDAQ), IPG Photonics ($IPGP, NASDAQ)

$HIMX – Himax Technologiesผลิตชิปประมวลผลภาพและชิปแสดงผล

เหมาะสำหรับหุ่นยนต์และอุปกรณ์ Edge AI ที่ต้องการประมวลผลอัจฉริยะโดยใช้พลังงานต่ำ

$ON – ON Semiconductor กำลังสร้างแพลตฟอร์ม Edge AI สำหรับหุ่นยนต์ หลังเข้าซื้อ $SYNA – Synaptics
รวมเทคโนโลยีด้านพลังงาน เซ็นเซอร์ การเชื่อมต่อ และ Edge AI ไว้ในระบบเดียว

$SYM – Symbotic ผู้เชี่ยวชาญด้านระบบอัตโนมัติในคลังสินค้า
ใช้หุ่นยนต์และซอฟต์แวร์เพื่อเพิ่มประสิทธิภาพการจัดเก็บและขนส่งสินค้า

$VPG – Vishay Precision Group พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์วัดแรงและระบบวัดความแม่นยำสูง
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์รับรู้แรงกด น้ำหนัก และการเคลื่อนไหวในโลกจริงได้แม่นยำขึ้น

$TSLA – Tesla เดิมพันกับหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid ผ่าน Optimus
ผสานฮาร์ดแวร์ AI ระบบขับเคลื่อนอัตโนมัติ และศักยภาพการผลิตขนาดใหญ่ของบริษัท

$CCXI – Churchill Capital Corp XI
เปิดโอกาสให้นักลงทุนเข้าถึง Agility Robotics
บริษัทผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid Digit ซึ่งเป็นหนึ่งในผู้เล่นที่โดดเด่นด้านหุ่นยนต์สำหรับคลังสินค้า

text:
$STM, STMicroelectronics. STM is one of Europe's largest chipmakers, and it builds its own chips end-to-end, from design all the way through its own factories. Its parts are the unglamorous but essential pieces inside almost everything — the sensors, power chips, and tiny "brains" (microcontrollers) inside cars, phones, factory machines, satellites, robots, and now AI data centers.

What makes STM unique is the sheer breadth: it holds a #1 or #2 position in an unusual number of separate chip categories at once (SiC power, MEMS sensors, STM32 microcontrollers), which lets a customer like NVIDIA source a whole system — sensors, brain, power, and communications — from one vendor. The reason it's suddenly interesting is that a company written off for two years as a "sleepy automotive chipmaker" just found a real AI growth engine: on June 2 it doubled its 2026 data-center revenue target to ~$1B (with a path to ~$2B in 2027), anchored by a multi-year AWS silicon-photonics deal, while sitting on ~$2.8B net cash. The investment case is a cyclical turnaround (Q1 revenue +23% YoY, up ~90%+ off February lows) layered on top of a genuine new secular driver, with the biggest names in tech — AWS, NVIDIA — as partners.

Thesis: STM spent two years being ignored as a boring car-chip company, but it just quietly turned into an AI company — its parts now go inside Amazon and NVIDIA's data centers, and that business is set to double two years in a row. The stock has already jumped a lot, so the easy money is gone, but the real question is whether its profit margins can climb back to normal as sales recover — if they do, the stock is still cheap for what it's about to earn. I'm watching the July 23 earnings to see if those margins are finally turning up, because that's the part the market hasn't fully believed yet.

text:
spent the morning reading the $LAZR filing. semi analysis teamed up with tema to launch a pure-play optics etf and honestly it makes sense. the memory bottleneck got DISK. now the data movement bottleneck gets its own wrapper.

top holding is lumentum at 14.6%. they make the lasers and transceivers that move data between racks at ai scale. second biggest position is literally cash at 11.4% which tells you this thing just launched and they're still deploying.

the interesting name here is aixtron at 10.5%. german compound semi equipment. mocvd deposition tools that build the substrates optical chips run on. axti at 9.7% is the substrate play itself. applied opto at 5.8% is the highest beta name in the basket.

goldman has the photonics market doing 9x between now and 2028. copper hits a wall. silicon photonics stops being a niche and becomes the thing everything rides on. same pattern we saw with memory when DISK launched.

the optics layer is where the next capacity crunch forms. watch the holdings more than the etf itself. the basket tells you where the spend is heading before the earnings do.

Fiber & Infrastructure Highlights – July 3, 2026

GoNetspeed announced a $43M expansion in Rahway, Clark, Linden, and Woodbridge, New Jersey for over 34,000 homes and businesses. Persistent fiber shortages continue with ribbon lead times at 60+ weeks (AI/data center priority) and loose tube into Q3 2026+; BABA-compliant glass remains limited. Corning’s hyperscaler deals (up to $6B with Meta for NC expansion including new Hickory facility; multi-billion with Amazon) and manufacturing ramps (Nvidia-funded plants) address demand. FiberLight’s $350M West Texas expansion (1,400 route miles, high-count dark fiber for AI) and total ~$500M regional investment continue. Ripple Fiber has multiple expansions (WA $250M for 200k+ locations, IL, AZ $80M, CA) with ongoing construction.

Vocus Sydney-Melbourne ducted long-haul (~A$500M, ready 2029), Lightpath NY metro (+265 miles for 2,400+ towers), Heartland Fiber Project, Big Fiber financing, and various BEAD/FTTH builds add to activity. Fiber vendors (Corning, Fujikura tripling capacity, STL, CommScope) scale high-count, multi-core, and photonics solutions amid AI’s 36x fiber density needs. FBA whitepaper on fiber-AI convergence and power/grid constraints highlight sustained physical layer momentum into 2026.

#FiberForge #FiberOptics #OSPISP

text:
Im not personally in photonics, i just share this info because it may be of use to someone. 

That said, i believe only a handful of names in this market are actually going to be successful LT (marvell, lumentum, coherent, etc). CPO is still early...you wont see meaningful revenue until 2027-2028. A lot of people are getting ahead and investing in overvalued photonics stocks, hence the sharp pullback its see the past few weeks. At some point the valuation will matter, and its mattering now.
@pequityresearch Excellent breakdown of the Silicon Photonics supply chain. Very informative and well-organized. Great work highlighting the key players across the ecosystem.

If you have better companies or sectors to buy, then selling half can definitely make sense.

I already have exposure to most sectors where I see major upside: defence, photonics, memory, and space. So overall, I'm quite happy with the distribution.

At most, I might rotate some direct exposure into robotics, but I'm still not fully convinced, especially on humanoids. Maybe I'm biased because of EU regulations...

text:
Sivers Semiconductors (https://t.co/8VMNAF5r3U / SIVEF) has strong long-term growth potential in high-margin photonics and wireless tech sectors, but faces near-term dilution pressure, execution risks, and valuation challenges following this news.

What the News Means
•Bootstrap Europe is converting its full $12M convertible loan (part of a $17M refinancing from Feb 2026) into 22,847,044 new ordinary shares at SEK 4.77 per share.

•This eliminates that debt (positive for the balance sheet and interest costs) and simplifies the capital structure.

•Dilution impact: With ~296 million shares outstanding pre-conversion, this adds ~7-8% more shares. It’s already anticipated (disclosed in Feb 2026, with conversion price at a premium back then), but it increases the float and can create selling pressure if Bootstrap sells shares.

•The stock jumped ~15% today (aligning with your “16+ plus”), likely because the conversion removes debt overhang and signals confidence from the lender (who is now a shareholder).

Short-Term Outlook (Next 3-12 Months)
•Positive drivers: The conversion cleans up the balance sheet. The company has a massive ~$800M opportunity pipeline (up 77% YTD as of Q1), with catalysts like product ramps in 2027 (LiDAR, SATCOM, fixed wireless, defense, and potential AI/data center optics). Q2 results are due around early August.15
•Headwinds: Significant dilution (this event + recent/ongoing directed share issues for funding). The stock is volatile and has pulled back from highs near SEK 110. High valuation (very elevated P/S multiples relative to current revenue) makes it sensitive to any delays or misses. Recent sector selloffs in photonics (e.g., CPO concerns) have added pressure.11
•Expect continued volatility. Bootstrap may sell gradually (or hold), but the increased share count could cap upside until positive news dominates.
Medium-to-Long-Term Future (2027+)
•Bull case (optimistic): Strong execution on the pipeline could drive ~30%+ annual revenue growth, with breakeven/profitability targeted for 2027. Key markets (AI optics, LiDAR production ramps, SATCOM/defense) have high potential. Analysts forecast sharp earnings improvement. A Nasdaq dual-listing could boost visibility and liquidity.1965
•Base case: Revenue scales with product ramps, but competition and execution risks (delays in customer adoption) remain. Dilution from this and other raises will mute per-share gains.
•Risks: Still pre-profit with cash burn, dependency on converting the pipeline into actual revenue, sector hype cycles, and further equity raises. High valuation leaves little room for error. $Sivef

Overall: This news is neutral-to-slightly positive fundamentally (debt reduction) but adds immediate dilution. The company’s future hinges on 2027+ ramps materializing in photonics/wireless. It’s a high-risk, high-reward growth story suited for investors who believe in the long-term AI/semiconductor tailwinds and can tolerate volatility. Not financial advice—do your own research, consider the full financials, and monitor upcoming reports. $Sive

@Beth_Kindig is bullish on Lumentum.

SemiAnalysis published a hit piece on the timing of CPO adoption while privately preparing to launch an institutional photonics ETF, in which Lumentum holds the largest weighting at 14.6%. Notably, $AAOI carries a 5.8% weighting while $COHR carries 2.95%.

🤔 🤔 🤔

text:
Why have I chosen to hold it long term instead of selling it at a local top?

1. Because the long term thesis is very strong. 

$SIVE is involved in some of the most important verticals for the new economy. Namely

- photonics (ai)
- lidar (robotics)
- wearable sensors
- RF (drones, space tech, 5g, 6g)

They already have customers for all this. They already have partnerships with the foundries.

2. Because we already have institutional validation and ownership. $JPM and Fidelity were already revealed in the fillings. 

Then the last fundraiser was oversubscribed by multiples from institutions. 

3. When you look at where other players in photonics alone are - you have $LITE at 50-70b $AAOI at 10b $COHR at 65b. 

Then there are also the RF players, the space players, the drones players, the Lidar players. 

Sivers is in all that. So obviously if they execute their valuation will be going towards 10b and beyond.

Sure it will take time, but if the institutions are buying in, that's enough validation for me. They make calculated moves and don't panic sell like retail dilettantes.

text:
Whenever $SIVE drops and you think of pressing that “sell” button, read this post.

Look…

Markets go through waves - up and down. Simple as that.

We are still early in photonics compared to other AI cycles. Volatility is expected and will continue.

It’s the nature of early-stage cycles and high-growth tech companies like $SIVE. This isn’t the stable phase yet.

At the end of the day, it comes down to conviction in your thesis and staying unmoved through the storm if you believe it will play out.

What I can say is…

Every time I look at $SIVE, it seems to be getting better, not worse.

What’s moving the price is macro uncertainty and fear, not anything negative about the company’s performance or progress.

In fact, they are taking the right steps forward. The day they stop, I’ll stop talking about them and sell. I promise.

After all the research and analysis over the last few months, I’m very bullish and convinced on $SIVE. Building your own conviction matters. Nothing has changed for me.

Holding all shares and adding more soon. So many times I wished I could have bought more below 50 SEK. Now it’s here. 👀

Let me present that to you all.

The Goldman Sachs report has a lot of nectar in it. Beyond the overall TAM expansion numbers for optical networks & CPO, a 10x TAM jump in just two years - from $15B to $154B - is no joke. CPO alone contributes $91B of that (59%). That’s already massive. 

But let’s dig in deeper...

Mass-volume production and mainstream adoption of connection speeds beyond 1.6T+ kicks off in H2 2026 and runs through H2 2028. Overall, CPO TAM totals ~$97B from 2026-2028. $JBL, a confirmed partner with $SIVE for their 1.6T programs, already gives them solid exposure on the transceiver side. Other partners are mentioned later on.

Optics is clearly preferred over copper for higher speeds, but the costs are higher too because the transceivers and lasers needed at this scale are expensive. That means more revenue for $SIVE - because $SIVE makes the CW lasers, which are the most widely adopted light source across optical modules for both scale-out and scale-up. You can see why a moat is building here. 

On top of that, there’s supply tightness for these lasers, which adds pricing power and improves margins. Goldman Sachs expects this tightness to continue through 2027, with possible relief only in H2 2028.

This isn’t the only bullish report. 

Bank of America also published their TAM projections for Optical Networking, reaching $87.7B by 2030 at a 40% CAGR. While it looks less aggressive, that’s mainly because BofA focuses more on the direct market for optical hardware components rather than the entire networking architecture.

$SIVE’s current opportunity pipeline sits at $453M, up 64% YoY. They did $31.21M in FY2025 revenue (almost 10% of the pipeline). 

Let’s assume they keep growing the opportunity pipeline roughly 2x by 2030. I say this because the TAM is expanding 10x, and everything lines up in their favor - from supply chain constraints & Tier-1 links to CW lasers being the most widely adopted.

That gives us roughly an ~$8B opportunity pipeline. At a conservative 12.5% execution rate into revenue, that’s $1B in revenue by 2030. At 40x P/S, you’re looking at a $40B company.

And $1B isn’t out of reach, because multiple revenue streams will come in beyond just photonics:

> Biosensing through $AAPL watches could bring in almost $130M in annual revenue alone at high-volume scale.

> SatCOM & defense is another big watchout, especially after the MOU signed. With the satellite market projected to grow 3-4x by 2030 and defense spending rising to adopt new tech (backed by governments), $SIVE is well positioned. Geopolitical tensions and catalysts like a potential SpaceX IPO only add fuel.

> Photonics will clearly make up the majority of revenue (well over 50%), but this diversification derisks the business and avoids over-reliance on just a few big orders.

Now the question is: how can such a small company become so big in a few years?

> A US listing will bring much more liquidity and institutional flow. It lets them expand capacity and go directly to Tier-1 customers instead of relying on indirect channels. They’ve already hired M&A specialists to possibly being able to scale beyond just being a laser supplier - producing their own transceivers, ELS modules, and other optical components, and acquiring new IP. This expands revenue per unit and their TAM. $MRVL is did the exact same thing with Celestial AI. Revenue in 2029 solely from that division is expected to bring them ~$1B.

> Geopolitical advantage isn’t being fully priced in yet. The US wants to reduce dependency on China and Asia for manufacturing. $SIVE, as a Swedish company building a stronger US base, is perfectly placed to ride the “American Dream” vision. The MOU confirms this. Presence in India also helps, as it’s becoming a semiconductor hub (see $MU’s recent fab).

> Never underestimate retail backing. Look at what it did for $PLTR and $HOOD. I see the same thing happening here - it brings validation, higher volumes and momentum. Marketing still matters, and retail is powerful for that.

So...

The company is doing all the right things. They’re executing in a TAM that’s clearly stacked in their favor to become the next $LITE, and they have a huge, growing retail fanbase behind them.

The vision is clear and ambitious, as the CEO himself said: to become “a very successful semiconductor company by 2030, recognized the same way some of the biggest companies out there are.”

Risks exist - the story might not play out perfectly. But even then, today’s ~$2B market cap still offers 10x potential by 2030. The real legacy upside sits at 15-20x.

After reading this, I don’t know what else the AI bubble and delusional bears need to believe.

Next time before claiming something, it would be wiser to some research. 😌

text:
View on sector. $Sive 

This is the key finding: it’s sector-wide, not SIVE-specific.
SECTOR CONTEXT (this changes the read materially)
AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics): dropped roughly 17% on July 2, with Coherent and Lumentum sinking around 10% in the same session — headlines describe this as a broad “photonics stocks reset.” A separate report also flags AAOI shares as sharply lower amid a “risk-off stretch for growth” and investors “rotating out of unprofitable tech.”
XFAB (X-FAB): search results are contradictory/stale across sources (one shows +47% “last session,” another shows -8.6% weekly, dates ranging from May to July) — data quality here isn’t reliable enough for me to give you a clean today-vs-yesterday read. I’d check this one directly on your IBKR/TradingView feed.

SIVE: closed 49 SEK, down from 60.75 on July 1 (which was itself already -3.8% that day, off a 63.15 prior close). So the stock has fallen roughly three consecutive sessions since the emission.

WHAT THIS MEANS FOR YOUR READ
The AAOI/Coherent/Lumentum move on the same days tells you something important: the CPO/photonics complex broadly de-rated this week, likely tied to a “risk-off” rotation out of high-multiple, unprofitable AI-infrastructure names — not a SIVE-specific loss of confidence.

That reframes your situation:
•Sector-wide component: growth/photonics multiple compression hitting AAOI, COHR, LITE simultaneously — this is macro/rotation, not fundamental to SIVE.
•SIVE-specific component: still real — the stock is trading below its own 700 MSEK emission price, which is a distinct, company-specific overhang (institutions underwater on their entry, no fresh lock-up on management past July 16).

Both things are true at once: broad photonics de-rating plus SIVE-specific dilution digestion. That’s a worse combination than either alone, but it does mean you shouldn’t read the SIVE drop as pure company-specific panic.

text:
$Sive break $50 critical level or psychology level. —  Today it closed at 49 SEK is now 14% below the emission price of 57 SEK, not just a brief dip below it. That changes the picture materially.

WHAT THIS TELLS YOU
1. The oversubscription didn’t hold as a floor. Institutions who paid 57 SEK are already underwater too. If the “smart money” validation thesis was going to show up as support, it should have shown up in the first 48 hours. It didn’t.

2. This is now a confidence problem, not just a dilution problem. A stock breaking meaningfully below its own primary offering price within days signals the market isn’t just digesting dilution — it’s questioning the raise itself, or de-risking ahead of something (Q2 earnings, sector rotation, general AI-infrastructure profit-taking).

3. Your position math: Entry 104 → 63 (late June) → now 49. That’s roughly a 53% drawdown from cost basis. This is no longer “a drawdown to manage” — it’s a thesis-under-real-stress situation.

WHAT TO WATCH NEXT, NOT SPECULATE ON
•Volume on the break below 57: heavy volume = capitulation/distribution; light volume = could be thin summer liquidity exaggerating the move.
•Sector context: check whether XFAB, AAOI, and other photonics/CPO peers are also down today — if it’s sector-wide, this is rotation, not SIVE-specific.
•Insider behavior into July 16: management lock-up expires then. Any signs of selling pressure building ahead of that date would be a real red flag on top of this.
•Whether 57 SEK flips from floor to resistance on any bounce attempt — that’s the technical tell for whether institutions are defending their entry or already cutting losses.
Emotionally and psychologically break point #Sive

text:
Couldn’t help sharing these words by $IONQ CEO:

“The science phase of our company was done. We’re now focused on engineering and manufacturing.”

$IONQ is just an example that makes it clear quantum companies will find a way to make logical qubits.

The challenge is scaling them up. This is what $IONQ has been up to:

1/ It acquired Oxford Ionics for electronic qubit control.

2/ It acquired Lightsynq to integrate photonic interconnects to link multiple quantum units.

3/ It is going to acquire Skywater to reduce the chip design cycle from 9 months to 2 months and gain control over its supply chain.

$IONQ has a clear roadmap toward building commercially useful quantum computers.
embedded_text:
Quantum is like a freight train coming after anyone who is not invested in hybrid classical and quantum workflows.

Hear more from our CEO @NiccoloDeMasi in a newly released interview from @FII_Institute1: https://t.co/cmnmSIGDMz

text:
$sive ⚡️

Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE) is a deep-tech company that designs and manufactures specialized microchips and lasers for ultra-fast, high-capacity communications.

Instead of using traditional electrical copper wiring—which creates heat and becomes a bottleneck at high speeds—Sivers builds high-power lasers and laser arrays that transmit data using light (silicon photonics).

While Photonics is the AI growth engine Sivers also designs radio frequency integrated circuits (RFICs) and beamforming antennas that operate on millimeter-wave (mmWave) frequencies.

Under 2b Market Cap this is a high risk and highly speculative play.  Current revenues/contracts and assest in no way make the current valuation.  Much of the valuation is still derived from a "potential pipeline" and "potential opportunity" but the potential upside is immense if they can execute.

$sive has run from 3sek to 100sek 🧐 
Wowsers, thats a 3000+% parabolic run.  
The current pullback is a natural part of the stock cycle and stocks that make these parabolic runs typically will retrace to .618 or 45sek or even more frequently .786 25sek.  

I missed the last run, as it had passed my buy targets and I NEVER chase but keen to enter here if she plays ball.
My intention is to scale in from 45sek to 30sek as this reduces risk and creates huge upside opportunity.

Below is my Framework score.

Sivers Semiconductors (SIVE / SIVEF)
Score: 73/100

Management & Vision  11/15
Credentialed CEO (Vathulya — NXP/Maxim/Nuvotronics, bought stock) + commercialization-weighted board refresh (Nideborn CFO, Svancar M&A, Thomson, Raj); chairman Bastani's GF network drove the GlobalFoundries deal. Capped by broad insider selling (~SEK 117M net, 12mo) + open Swedish probe, moves to 12/13 on confirmation of no wrongdoing.

Moat  12/15
Confirmed InP substrate scarcity (industry chokepoint, 6-inch pricing +250%); genuinely hard manufacturing; Glasgow fab (2M+ tests/mo, etched-facet); GF reference design + Ayar Labs supply (OCI/NVIDIA). Capped because "at scale" is still prospective. Moves to 14 on proof of scale.

TAM 14/15
InP/CPO/NPO demand structurally huge; two-division (Photonics + Wireless) exposure; GFS silicon photonics ~$25B by 2030. Unchanged.

Probable Upside  10/13
BU ~195%. Targets: 2026 SEK 70 (+4.5%), 2028 SEK 150 (+124%), 2030 SEK 320 (+377%). Weighted to 2030 execution. Targets self-built, not sell-side. Moves to 13 on contracts and proof of scale.

Revenue Quality  5/12
Confirmed facts : auditor-driven restatement widened losses, going-concern consideration, Q1 revenue -22% YoY, adj. EBITDA -13.8M SEK. Can move up fast on a clean Aug 6 print.

Conviction Gap  8/10
Market pricing max uncertainty; JPMorgan 5.25% entry post-controversy; options listing (Jul 6) unlocks institutional access. 

Risk-Adjusted  4/10
Forced/dilutive raise floating (53.8M mandate, ~15%), ~230% annualized vol, convertible at ~10.85%, thin cash (~SEK 26.6M vs -49.2M quarterly burn). Absorbs Down+TA.

Geo-Political  9/10
US/Sweden/UK — allied, stable, NDAA-friendly, no China domicile. Capped only by live Swedish regulatory probe.

text:
Serenity points out that no one should pay attention to technical analysis charts

Considering them nothing more than simple lines drawn to persuade others to sell

They highlight that $SIVE secured $70M in urgent financing to boost high volume production and lock in foundry capacity

Right at the same time companies like $JBL $POET and $AEVA are preparing short term volume ramps

They show their highest conviction in $SIVE as a long position in the photonics sector, with further production ramps from $GFS Ayar, and others lined up for 2027

Additionally, the confirmed intention to list on NASDAQ in the coming quarters shows that everything is aligning favorably

You wanted to buy $SIVE at a "discount" at 90 SEK, but you’re scared to buy the exact same company at 50 SEK

Think twice, long $SIVE

text:
People forget that just as $SIVE has dropped 50% in a matter of days, it can easily pump 200% in a few months

And I firmly believe that will happen as long as the thesis is allowed to play out

Fortunately, the money I invest is capital I don't depend on

When I was sitting on 200% returns, I didn't touch a single euro, and now that I'm 15% down, I'm still not touching anything

That’s what allows me to just hold through it

It's undeniable that seeing the portfolio drop this much hurts

And it's undeniable that at least half of the drawdown I've taken could have been avoided; it was due to my own mistakes

Mistakes from which I have learned, a lot

That’s why I’m not worried

AI isn't going anywhere

Memory, like $MU ,is here to stay, especially with a shortage projected for years

And photonics, like $SIVE, isn't dead, no matter how much you see all of FinX claiming it is

It’s very easy to talk like that right now because everything is crashing hard, but where were those posts when everything was ripping 10% daily?

Literally nothing has changed; it’s just overstimulated people rotating like headless chickens, bouncing between sectors because they don't understand a thing

It's only a matter of time before that money flows back twice as hard as it left

As soon as the sector leaders report earnings or clarify timelines regarding certain technologies, everything will bounce back

I am absolutely sure of it

Long $MU and $SIVE

text:
Serenity criticized the sharp drop in semiconductor stocks following a Bloomberg report about potential AI capex cuts by $META 

A UBS analyst pointed out that this isn't even new information, adding that $META wouldn't be signing neocloud contracts worth over $48B if they were truly scaling back capacity

They compared this overreaction to previous misleading Bloomberg reports, calling the market "stupid" for selling off so irrationally

I completely agree, this is a completely unjustified sell off

I know I might not be back at all-time highs in a month or two, but I should at least bounce back to halfway from where I was

The main issue is that I’m running out of cash, and I’m right on the edge of losses, which honestly makes it tough

But I try to remind myself that the thesis just needs time to play out

Even if I dip into the red right now, if my price targets for $MU and $SIVE are met, I could still end the year with 200% to 400% returns

It’s just a matter of time

There is no bubble here, and this has absolutely nothing to do with the dot-com crash

There are insane, massive earnings backing it all up, from $MU to $NVDA

On the other hand, $SIVE needed a correction

I admit I got greedy and never took profits

But you have to realize that everything remains the same, or even better, than it was at 4 SEK

Photonics isn't dead, even if it feels like the end of the world looking at semiconductor stocks today and throughout this week

There's nothing left to do but hold

My conviction is worth more than my current returns

Long $SIVE and $MU

text:
— Kyber NVL144, the flagship 2027 rack, delayed 12+ months to 2028. The PCB midplane can’t be manufactured at scale
— The backup architecture (NVL72x2) — cancelled
— The 4-die Rubin Ultra was already dead: packaging physically warps at that size. The 2-die survivor delivers roughly half the performance per package
— Optical scale-up (CPO) won’t arrive until Feynman

This is not one product slipping. It’s two physical walls — packaging AND interconnect — hitting in the same cycle. The “new rack every year” promise is what’s breaking.

WINNERS

— $GOOGL / $AVGO — the only proven alternative scale-up architecture on earth (optical switching, running in production for years). The 2027 window belongs to the TPU ecosystem
— $AMD — gets a window. A window is not a win. Execution at rack scale is unproven
— Pluggable optics: $COHR, $FN, $AAOI — CPO was the sword hanging over pluggables. It just got sheathed for another generation. The recent photonics selloff shot the wrong victim
— Copper: $APH, $CRDO — copper stays in the rack longer than anyone modeled
— Neoclouds: $NBIS, $CRWV — slower chip cadence means GPU fleets obsolete slower. The depreciation treadmill every bear cites just lost a year

LOSERS

— $NVDA — not revenue. They’ll sell every mature-gen rack they can make. What’s damaged is the moat premium: NVLink supremacy and the annual cadence ARE the multiple
— CPO pure plays: $POET, Sivers — the entire thesis is co-packaged optics adoption timing. That timing just moved right by a full generation. The biggest losers (Ayar Labs, Celestial AI) are private — you can’t even trade them
— $LITE — the trap. CPO laser story delayed, pluggable laser business extended. Net wash. Not the short people will think it is

THE $MU QUESTION

Do the math before you touch the sell button. Old Kyber rack: 144 packages x 16 HBM stacks = 2,304. New 2+2 config: 288 packages x 8 stacks = 2,304. Bit demand per rack: identical. What died is the “HBM per GPU doubles forever” narrative — a multiple story, not a bits story. And a flatter performance curve means buyers need MORE units for the same compute, in a market that’s supply-constrained until 2028.

Neutral on fundamentals. If it does trade down on the headline, then that gap will be called opportunity.

The nuance everyone will miss: NVIDIA can still hit rack-level compute targets with 2+2. Parity — bought with twice the silicon. The spec sheet survives. Cost per FLOP doesn’t.

The only question for 2027: can anyone exploit the gap before Feynman closes it? Exactly one company has a working alternative today.

Not financial advice.

text: https://t.co/eJZpNXVDA8

Bullish $SIVE: AMD is building CPO for the MI500 (2027) through GlobalFoundries, bypassing NVIDIA’s TSMC packaging bottleneck entirely. Since June, Sivers’ laser arrays are integrated into GF’s silicon photonics reference designs and SCALE CPO platform. If AMD’s optical scale-up bet materializes at GF, Sivers lasers are in the supply chain. NVIDIA stumbling widens exactly the window AMD and its photonics partners need.

Bullish $AAOI: every year CPO slips at NVIDIA is another year pluggable transceivers remain the workhorse of AI datacenters. Delays don’t destroy optical demand, they redirect capital back to pluggables and NPO. That’s AAOI’s core business. The market dumped it 17% on the delay headline without asking who actually benefits from it.

One company wins if CPO accelerates outside NVIDIA. The other wins because it’s delayed inside NVIDIA. Both win from the same news.

Delays don’t kill demand. They redistribute it.

RILEY SAYS AMAZON'S RNG & OPENAI'S MRC PROTOCOLS POSE MAJOR STRUCTURAL RISK FOR OPTICAL INFRASTRUCTURE / "PHOTONICS" STOCKS, NAMELY APPLIED OPTOELECTRONICS $AAOI Full comments: "Following the OFC conference in March 2025, the AI data center boom triggered an unprecedented optical supercycle. As demonstrated by the parabolic re-rating of optical infrastructure stocks such as Lumentum ($LITE – Buy, $1,142 PT ) and Coherent ($COHR – Neutral, $309 PT ), the prevailing investment thesis assumed an aggressive, compounding multiplier: scaling AI clusters meant building deeper, multi-tiered networks (3-tier or 4-tier Clos topologies), exponentially driving up transceiver demand per unit of compute. This thesis, however, is hitting a structural wall. Just a year after the 2025 explosion, the simultaneous market introduction of Amazon’s ($AMZN, NR) Resilient Network Graphs (RNG) and OpenAI’s Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) protocol marks a major pivot toward network flattening across both training and inference workloads. Microsoft ($MSFT, NR) and Oracle ($ORCL, NR) are actively integrating the open-source MRC protocol into their frontier AI training supercomputing clusters—such as ORCL’s massive GB200 Abilene site—to execute sender-controlled packet spraying and bypass physical network bottlenecks. Concurrently, AMZN is deploying its quasi-random RNG architecture across its general-purpose cloud fabric. While RNG is isolated from back-end training clusters, it serves as the primary backbone for scale-out generative AI inference, data ingestion, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Crucially, as mature AI models transition from training to mass inference, hyperscaler capex is shifting heavily in favor of these front-end, scale-out environments. Because RNG replaces traditional, active multi-tiered aggregation switches with passive optical ShuffleBoxes, it reduces active networking equipment by over 60%. Consequently, as AI workloads migrate to inference, this architectural deflation loops directly into the broader optical market, stripping out switch-to-switch optical links and dismantling the legacy transceiver-to-compute multiplier. For the transceiver market, this dual-front flattening represents a severe structural headwind that will sharply decelerate the sector's long-term TAM expansion, since these technologies reduce structural transceiver counts by 40% to 50%. We believe Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI – Neutral, $129 PT) will be especially vulnerable to this development. AMZN and ORCL—the primary network provider to OpenAI—are expected to be the anchor customers for the 800G/1.6T transceivers forecast to drive AAOI's quarterly revenue to ~$1B by 2H27. As ORCL deflates training-cluster transceiver ratios via MRC, and AMZN strips active transceiver requirements from inference rollouts via RNG, AAOI's core revenue growth engines face massive structural risk.""}}}, "rest_id": "2074071756109656328", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "353961", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2074282003319452014", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "25", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$sive is real!

우리에게 생소할 수 있지만, 글로벌 데이터센터, 클라우드, 인프라 업계에서는 매우 높은 신뢰도와 영향력을 가진 대표적인 전문 B2B 미디어인 ‘Data Center Frontier'라는 곳에서,

AI 클러스터가 거대해짐에 따라 기존 구리선 기반 연결의 한계를 극복하기 위해 광학 기술 및 실리콘 포토닉스를 데이터센터 인프라에 도입해야 한다는 내용을 다룸. 

그리고 이 기사는 6시간 전에 올라온 기사.

이 기사는 데이터 센터 프론티어의 시니어 에디터인 David Chernicoff가 작성했으며,

중요한 건 $sive 관련 내용이 ‘굵직하게’ 포함됨. 

총 5가지 내용 중에 심지어 한 부분을 차지함. 

(원문 발췌)
“Those developments are now being reinforced by a broader series of moves across the optical ecosystem:
- Corning’s major AI infrastructure partnerships with NVIDIA and Amazon,
- GlobalFoundries’ push into co-packaged optics,
- Sivers’ laser-array collaboration with GlobalFoundries, $sive
- Wiwynn’s co-packaged optics demonstration at Computex,
- Credo’s acquisition of DustPhotonics, and emerging near-packaged optical interconnect designs from LightSpeed Photonics.”

기사의 $sive 관련 세부내용으로는,

1. $sive와 글로벌 파운드리스 $gfs 와의 레이저 어레이 협력 명시

- Sivers의 레이저 어레이(Laser arrays)는 글로벌파운드리의 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼을 기반으로 제작된 레퍼런스 디자인(참조 설계)에 통합되며, GF의 SCALE 광학 엔진 솔루션으로 제공될 예정. 

2. Wiwynn의 CPO 안의 Ayar labs 명시

- 알다시피 Ayar labs의 SuperNova에 $sive 가 들어감

이 두 내용을 이런 미디어의 기사에서 명시했다는 건 $sive 가 진짜라는 걸 간접적으로 확인해주는 것이지 않을까?

이 데이터 센터 프론티어라는 미디어는, 

독자층이 주로 구글, 마이크로소프트, 메타, AWS 같은 하이퍼스케일러(Hyperscaler)의 인프라 엔지니어링 임원, 데이터센터 아키텍트, 설계·건설 책임자, 그리고 데이터센터 리츠(REITs) 투자가들이라고 하고, 

독자의 약 50% 이상이 C-Level(경영진)이나 VP, 디렉터급으로 구성되어 있어, 실질적인 인프라 투자와 기술 도입에 중요한 이정표가 된다고 함. 

기사 원문 링크는 댓글 참고

text:
Be careful on $AAOI.

There's a crazy amount of resentment from institutions such as B. Riley who MISSED the stock and were bearish as low as $15.

Now making a massive intellectual leap on a nonsensical optical short thesis and literally name dropping $AAOI out of bitterness? Spite? For the virality?

You really can't make up the misinformation and FUD in photonics right now it's just unbelievable. Earnings should put everyone in their place very soon. Nothing has changed in the fact that there isn't enough photonics tech in the world today and won't be for years.

Everyone in my paid Substack + Discord knows I've been buying $AAOI all the way down from $160 in my trading portfolio, most recently loading at $114. I also increased $AAOI in my "Photonics Is Next" portfolio today.

No asymmetry without FUD - NAWF.

RILEY SAYS AMAZON'S RNG & OPENAI'S MRC PROTOCOLS POSE MAJOR STRUCTURAL RISK FOR OPTICAL INFRASTRUCTURE / "PHOTONICS" STOCKS, NAMELY APPLIED OPTOELECTRONICS $AAOI Full comments: "Following the OFC conference in March 2025, the AI data center boom triggered an unprecedented optical supercycle. As demonstrated by the parabolic re-rating of optical infrastructure stocks such as Lumentum ($LITE – Buy, $1,142 PT ) and Coherent ($COHR – Neutral, $309 PT ), the prevailing investment thesis assumed an aggressive, compounding multiplier: scaling AI clusters meant building deeper, multi-tiered networks (3-tier or 4-tier Clos topologies), exponentially driving up transceiver demand per unit of compute. This thesis, however, is hitting a structural wall. Just a year after the 2025 explosion, the simultaneous market introduction of Amazon’s ($AMZN, NR) Resilient Network Graphs (RNG) and OpenAI’s Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) protocol marks a major pivot toward network flattening across both training and inference workloads. Microsoft ($MSFT, NR) and Oracle ($ORCL, NR) are actively integrating the open-source MRC protocol into their frontier AI training supercomputing clusters—such as ORCL’s massive GB200 Abilene site—to execute sender-controlled packet spraying and bypass physical network bottlenecks. Concurrently, AMZN is deploying its quasi-random RNG architecture across its general-purpose cloud fabric. While RNG is isolated from back-end training clusters, it serves as the primary backbone for scale-out generative AI inference, data ingestion, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Crucially, as mature AI models transition from training to mass inference, hyperscaler capex is shifting heavily in favor of these front-end, scale-out environments. Because RNG replaces traditional, active multi-tiered aggregation switches with passive optical ShuffleBoxes, it reduces active networking equipment by over 60%. Consequently, as AI workloads migrate to inference, this architectural deflation loops directly into the broader optical market, stripping out switch-to-switch optical links and dismantling the legacy transceiver-to-compute multiplier. For the transceiver market, this dual-front flattening represents a severe structural headwind that will sharply decelerate the sector's long-term TAM expansion, since these technologies reduce structural transceiver counts by 40% to 50%. We believe Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI – Neutral, $129 PT) will be especially vulnerable to this development. AMZN and ORCL—the primary network provider to OpenAI—are expected to be the anchor customers for the 800G/1.6T transceivers forecast to drive AAOI's quarterly revenue to ~$1B by 2H27. As ORCL deflates training-cluster transceiver ratios via MRC, and AMZN strips active transceiver requirements from inference rollouts via RNG, AAOI's core revenue growth engines face massive structural risk.""}}}, "rest_id": "2074071756109656328", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "353961", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2074273679697961422", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "102180", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
An ETF shop just filed a pure-play photonics ETF. That tells you as much about the top as it does about the opportunity.

$LYTE is Roundhill's new Photonics and Optics ETF. It is filed with the SEC but not trading yet. When it launches it will hold the optics pure-plays powering AI data centers: names like Lumentum, Coherent, POET, AAOI, IPG, and Ciena.

The pitch writes itself. If you believe in the photonics buildout but you cannot say whether $LITE, $COHR, or $POET wins, you own the group, not the single-name risk. That is a good argument for the basket.

But two things are worth saying out loud.

First, both the ETF and the single names are falling knives right now. This is a theme-wide de-rating. LITE knifed lower, AXTI is down 54% off its high, the whole optics complex is bleeding together. An ETF spreads single-name risk. It does nothing when the entire group falls. And LYTE is not even technically diversified. Its own filing calls it non-diversified, a concentrated basket of a handful of optics names.

Second, thematic ETFs tend to launch near tops. Roundhill built this because its DRAM ETF was a smash, hitting $1B in 10 days. Then memory crashed. An issuer racing to package a hot theme right as it corrects is a yellow flag, not a green light.

The move is not ETF or stock. It is neither, yet. Wait for the optics group to base, then use both: the ETF as the diversified core once it trades, the single name as the tactical piece you can sell puts on.

Not financial advice.

text:
He called $SIVE | $MU | $SNDK | $NBIS | $AXTI

These aren't random picks.

They're all solving critical bottlenecks in the AI trade:

Memory
Photonics
Neoclouds

The pieces of the supply chain that have to exist for the entire stack to work.

That's the difference between someone actually doing homework and someone just drawing lines on a chart.

On 6/2, my optical basket peaked at 123.5% all-time returns, achieving those gains in under 6 months, as computed by Autopilot's software. For context, the fund achieved this return holding a diverse basket of stocks, an incredible outcome for retail investors passively following. I'm extremely proud of our work on this product. There exists no optical ETF currently for retail investors despite a rush by issuers to cash in on the hype. My picks were launched back in February, providing retail investors a 6 month window to front run the category and get staked into the next AI bottleneck without the complexity of individual stock picking. Despite the recent sell off, the portfolio is still up 45% year to date, roughly 4.5x the S&P 500. $LAZR and $LYTE are two ETFs going to launch soon by major players, which should attract billions in AUM. However, I believe my allocations are superior and that my picks will outperform them with lower fees. So where are we now? Recently, the optical sector has gotten slammed based in my view mainly on sentiment, institutional profit taking, and disputed rumors from a well-known research firm on delays to next-gen architecture. Photonics Is Next is down 29.6% in the last month and 16.8% in just the last week. text:
We are seeking a highly motivated and enthusiastic Electronics Engineer to join our team and contribute to our mission in accelerating the growth and adoption of integrated photonics technologies.

▫️Job Title: Electronics Engineer
▫️Job Type: Full-time
▫️Salary: £50k – £70k pa., depending on experience
▫️Location: Cambridge, East of England, England
▫️Start Date: August 2026 or ASAP

📍Visa Sponsorship is available to those already in the UK 🇬🇧 

Qualifications 
✅️ Degree in Electronic Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Physics, or a related discipline.
✅️ Strong software development skills for embedded programming in C.
✅️ Python/MATLAB experience for measurement automation, data analysis, test and verification.
✅️ Commitment to facilitate effective cross-team knowledge transfer through clear documentation.

Apply here: https://t.co/Fq5Hqn0QQr

Please note: This Job is open to only those currently in the UK. Those outside the UK should not apply 

Please share this with others in the UK who may be Interested

🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩
 30-Second Stock Market Review
 🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥  

1️⃣ $META up 3% on a positive SemiAnalysis report stating that capex for 2027 is likely to remain intact, as the AI thesis remains solid. 
2️⃣ $BE up 8% amid ongoing volatility after the company recently announced a $25B framework from Brookfield to expand AI infrastructure. 
3️⃣ $AMD up 6% after Goldman Sachs raised its price target to $640. 
4️⃣ $IREN up 13% as AI compute and crypto stocks rallied. 
5️⃣ $TSLA up 6% amid recent production and delivery figures, along with momentum in robotics and physical AI stocks. 
6️⃣ $TSM up 4% on reports that the company is expected to raise its full-year revenue outlook to nearly 40% growth, up from 30%.
7️⃣ $CRDO up 10% as stocks in the optics and photonics sectors had relief rallies on Monday. 
8️⃣ $RKLB down 7% as space-themed stocks traded broadly lower. 
9️⃣ $DELL up 4% on positive comments from President Trump, urging consumers to buy the company’s products. 
🔟 $WULF up 4% on news that the company executed a 20-year lease deal with Anthropic at its data campus site in Kentucky. Also, the company signed an agreement to sell a 50.1% stake in the Abernathy joint venture to an investor group.

Market News

🆕 Bitcoin rose to nearly $64K on Monday after Trump made positive comments on crypto. 
🆕 Semiconductor stocks rallied after a nearly two-week slump. 

Upcoming Events 7/7/26

➡️ ADP Employment Change Weekly
➡️ 3-Year Note Auction
➡️ Earnings: $PENG, $EPAC, $KRUS, $SAR

*Information is from sources believed to be reliable. CenterPoint Securities cannot guarantee the accuracy or completeness of such information. This material should not be viewed as a recommendation to buy or sell any securities or to adopt any investment strategy.

text:
2026 PORTFOLIO UPDATE  Performance Metrics 

June 2026     :    6.39%
Harris YTD     : 45.79% 
S&P500 YTD: 12.84%  

My portfolio currently has 9 stocks: $nbis, $sofi, $mu, $aaoi, $asts, $oust, $pltr, $nflx, $onds

Currently 6/9 stocks in green, lagging only $sofi (who else!), $aaoi and $nflx.

Month Recap: It wasn't an easy month. It had a lot of ups and downs, and I'm really surprised that we closed the month in green. At one point I was -9%, but with the huge pump the last few days of the month, we managed to close in profit.

I will write down some thoughts of mine per each holding.
$nbis: This is the stock I have high conviction on. That doesn't mean I didn't trade it. I sold and bought quite a lot of times to make some profits and to initiate other positions. I've held it for one year now.
$sofi: I hold it from last year. Still in red, but I managed to bring my average down to $18.7, so I'm pretty close to breaking even. I love their CEO (@anthonynoto, send me a hat) as I believe he makes an extremely good job. The company is progressing really well, and the stock price will follow eventually.
$mu: I used to have SK Hynix, and I switched to have exposure to US markets. That's all. Same philosophy. Memory is in deep need.
$aaoi: A big player in photonics. All sector had a bad month. It will recover (I'm -14%), I'm not afraid.
$asts: I started buying when $spcx launched all the way to the bottom. I'm in green, I have a good average, and I believe a lot. Nothing more to say.
$oust: You see what happened last days with Physical AI and robots. Initially I bought in Aprin around $23 and sold at $36 for quick profit. Now I'm long here. Huge profit atm, I expect a pullback to take a breather.
$pltr & $nflx: I wanted exposure to both companies and I was looking for the opportunity. When $pltr dropped below 200week ema, I bought it and I have averaged. Netflix (last stock in red) gives me more opportunities for DCAing.
$onds: It was on my radar. I have issues with their CEO and his tactics (dilution and pumping), but at $7.9 it was a bargain and there was a huge disconnect between the company and the stock price. It's not a long-term position. When it reaches $10-$12, I planned to trim it.

Thanks for staying up to the end! If you enjoyed it, drop a 👋 emoji to see who read it.

Consider following me in order to be together in that journey. Today I reached 100k portfolio, so that's a huge milestone to celebrate!

text:
Half of the year is overwith. 

I have been fairly inactive over the last month as I spend time reflecting on the year, analyzing my holdings and seeing new opportunities for 2H. 

If I compartmentalize the year by months, I outperformed the majority in months 1-3 by having a defensive portfolio 25’ that was primary legacy manufacturers like $vrt $cat $gev. From April -> May I slightly underperformed, by not being able to pivot fast enough into emerging themes like memory and semis, only getting exposure to photonics through $aaoi. 

May -> present was OK, although my heavy positioning in $spcx ancillaries pulled performance down. Looking ahead I see no reason to 2H 26’ to be any different than what we have seen for the last few years. 

As I’m writing this I see Burry is now short my second largest holding caterpillar and I kindly ask him to accept my ballbag as I maintain bullish on the resurgence of heavy machinery and manufacturing names amidst an extremely pro America/infrastructure administration.

text:
Here's the post:

If I could only watch four layers in optical interconnect, ranked by bottleneck severity:
$COHR $LITE $FN $ANET

Nvidia just spent $6 billion locking down optical supply between March and June, and the market still thinks this is about transceivers.

The real story is InP substrates and laser chips — the stuff 20 layers upstream from what most people are tracking. Here's how I'd rank the supply chain by choke point plus earnings visibility over the next 12-24 months.

1. InP materials and epitaxy — the actual valve on compute scale. $COHR and $LITE aren't just CPO plays. They control high-purity indium phosphide epitaxy and high-speed laser IP that physically cannot be replaced in 800G/1.6T modules. Intel's CEO said it outright: InP is the non-negotiable material for solving chip-to-chip bandwidth. Nvidia locked each of them with separate $2 billion commitments and long-term offtake agreements. The Street prices them as module vendors. They're actually material oligopolists with compounding pricing power through every speed iteration.

2. CPO laser engines — the leverage on every GPU watt. $LITE and $COHR again, but now wearing their co-packaged optics hat. CPO moves the photonics inside the package, right next to the switch ASIC or GPU. That requires advanced packaging plus high-power lasers that don't cook themselves. Supply is sewn up. Goldman just put the scale-up interconnect TAM at $154 billion, and most of that growth is inside the rack and inside the super-pod, not between data centers. CPO sits in the middle of that build-out. Earnings inflection is 2026-2028 when hyperscalers move from pilots to volume. Risk is simple: if Nvidia capex wobbles, these names will gap hard because the trade is already crowded.

3. Module assembly and high-mix manufacturing — where cash flow actually prints. $FN is the TSMC of optical: low-drama contract manufacturing for every major module and CPO vendor. Margins are thinner than the chip guys, but revenue visibility is the cleanest in the whole chain because hyperscale deployment schedules are locked 18 months out. TrendForce just lifted 2025-2026 AI transceiver revenue from $16.5 billion to $26 billion, up 57% year-over-year. FN captures that with zero demand risk and no inventory. It's the only name here where you can model the next eight quarters with a straight face.

4. Switch and networking integration — the layer everyone already owns. $ANET is the consensus optical AI trade. Data center switching tied to optical back-end is a known quantity. Multiples reflect it. I'd rather own the three above because the market hasn't priced the material and assembly bottleneck the same way it's priced the switch attach rate.

The non-consensus bit: A-share money has been rotating from NAND and legacy semis into InP names for two quarters, but U.S. allocators still think optical is a 2025 story. It's a 2026-2028 margin story, and the earnings gap between upstream material plays and downstream module integrators is about to widen hard. I'm overweight the top two on that list until CPO production data forces multiple expansion.

The real story is InP substrates and laser chips — the stuff 20 layers upstream from what most people are tracking. Here's how I'd rank the supply chain by choke point plus earnings visibility over the next 12-24 months. 1. InP materials and epitaxy — the actual valve on compute scale. $COHR and $LITE aren't just CPO plays. They control high-purity indium phosphide epitaxy and high-speed laser IP that physically cannot be replaced in 800G/1.6T modules. Intel's CEO said it outright: InP is the non-negotiable material for solving chip-to-chip bandwidth. Nvidia locked each of them with separate $2 billion commitments and long-term offtake agreements. The Street prices them as module vendors. They're actually material oligopolists with compounding pricing power through every speed iteration. 2. CPO laser engines — the leverage on every GPU watt. $LITE and $COHR again, but now wearing their co-packaged optics hat. CPO moves the photonics inside the package, right next to the switch ASIC or GPU. That requires advanced packaging plus high-power lasers that don't cook themselves. Supply is sewn up. Goldman just put the scale-up interconnect TAM at $154 billion, and most of that growth is inside the rack and inside the super-pod, not between data centers. CPO sits in the middle of that build-out. Earnings inflection is 2026-2028 when hyperscalers move from pilots to volume. Risk is simple: if Nvidia capex wobbles, these names will gap hard because the trade is already crowded. 3. Module assembly and high-mix manufacturing — where cash flow actually prints. $FN is the TSMC of optical: low-drama contract manufacturing for every major module and CPO vendor. Margins are thinner than the chip guys, but revenue visibility is the cleanest in the whole chain because hyperscale deployment schedules are locked 18 months out. TrendForce just lifted 2025-2026 AI transceiver revenue from $16.5 billion to $26 billion, up 57% year-over-year. FN captures that with zero demand risk and no inventory. It's the only name here where you can model the next eight quarters with a straight face. 4. Switch and networking integration — the layer everyone already owns. $ANET is the consensus optical AI trade. Data center switching tied to optical back-end is a known quantity. Multiples reflect it. I'd rather own the three above because the market hasn't priced the material and assembly bottleneck the same way it's priced the switch attach rate. The non-consensus bit: A-share money has been rotating from NAND and legacy semis into InP names for two quarters, but U.S. allocators still think optical is a 2025 story. It's a 2026-2028 margin story, and the earnings gap between upstream material plays and downstream module integrators is about to widen hard. I'm overweight the top two on that list until CPO production data forces multiple expansion."}}}, "rest_id": "2072048464947478850", "source": "Buffer", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "632", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
This 🎯roadmap🎯 for data-center interconnect speeds is also, in many ways, a roadmap for #siliconphotonics.  As data rates move higher, the industry will need more integrated optical solutions, including near-package optics and co-packaged optics. That is where #siliconphotonics becomes increasingly relevant.
I adapted this roadmap based on analysis from an investment bank, which states that it is built on company disclosures as well as its own research.
It is interesting to observe how the upgrade cycles are overlapping. According to this analysis 400G is already moving into decline, 800G is becoming the mainstream workhorse, 1.6T is entering mass-volume territory, and 3.2T is already visible before 1.6T has fully settled. Furthermore, 800G may remain mainstream for only around six quarters. That is shorter than the typical 2–4 year cadence often expected for a mainstream optical transceiver generation. This points to a compressed innovation cadence for the industry. Qualification, yield learning, supply-chain readiness, thermal design, packaging, DSP, and test infrastructure all need to mature faster than before. Only the 💪fittest can survive this race. My view is that integrated photonics is entering a sustained innovation cycle, driven by the bandwidth, power, density, and packaging pressures of AI data-center infrastructure. 
🚀 #siliconphotonics

text: 6G's whole pitch rides on terahertz, and terahertz is brutal to actually build antennas for.
