text:
Russia ITMO University: Optical Tractor Beam with Metasurfaces and United States: Boeing's Magnetic Tractor Beam Patent

#ufotwitter

It's fascinating milestone in the field of modern nanophotonics. Published in ACS Photonics in late 2018 by physicists from ITMO University, the research details how hyperbolic metasurfaces can be used to generate stable optical pulling forces—commonly referred to as an "optical tractor beam."

To understand how this functions, it helps to look at the mechanics of standard radiation pressure and contrast it with how these specialized, engineered surfaces flip that mechanic on its head.

The Problem with Normal Light

Under ordinary conditions, when a laser beam strikes a small dielectric particle, the photons transfer their linear momentum to the object. This creates a standard scattering or radiation pressure that pushes the particle away from the light source, along the direction of propagation.

Creating a "tractor beam" requires generating a negative optical force (a pulling force) that acts against the global momentum of the incoming light, drawing the particle backward toward the source.

How Hyperbolic Metasurfaces Change the Mechanics

A hyperbolic metasurface is an ultra-thin, artificially engineered material characterized by extreme anisotropy. This means its electromagnetic properties (specifically its permittivity, have different signs along different spatial axes—for example, acting like a metal  in one direction and like a dielectric in another.

When a small particle is placed close to this surface and illuminated by a standard unstructured light source (like a plane wave), the interaction relies on three core phenomena:

High Density of States & Asymmetric Scattering: The hyperbolic nature of the substrate opens up highly localized, high-density electromagnetic interaction channels (both surface plasmons and volumetric waves within the substrate). The presence of the particle scatterer near the interface selectively excites these asymmetrical near-field modes.

Momentum Conservation and Recoil: Because the substrate allows light to scatter preferentially into these highly confined, high-momentum modes directed forward along or into the surface, the law of conservation of linear momentum requires a balance. To compensate for the forward momentum of the tailored scattering channels, the particle experiences a net mechanical recoil in the opposite direction.

Broadband Pulling: Unlike older optical trapping configurations that require complex, actively modulated phase holograms or spatial light modulators (SLMs) to sculpt a specific beam geometry (like a solenoid or Bessel beam), this model utilizes the intrinsic dispersion of the substrate itself. This allows for stable optical pulling forces over a significantly broader spectral range using simpler incident light fields.

Why This Mattered

Prior to this approach, pulling particles with light required highly complex, bulky optical setups that restricted spatial movement and demanded high laser power, which could damage biological samples or nanoscale structures. By shifting the engineering challenge from the light source to the material environment (the metasurface), this framework opened up new avenues for nanoscale manipulation, chip-based sorting of cells or macromolecules, and nanoscale fluidic self-organization.

United States: Boeing's Magnetic Tractor Beam Patent

This is an interesting find. You’re looking at US Patent US6634603B2, titled "Magnetic dipole tractor beam control system", filed by The Boeing Company back in 2001 and granted in 2003.

While the phrase "tractor beam" instantly evokes sci-fi, the actual engineering framework here is grounded in classic electromagnetic field manipulation. Here is a breakdown of what this patent actually describes, how it works, and its intended application.

The Core Concept: How It Works

Unlike fictional tractor beams that use exotic energy to pull objects across massive distances, this system relies on the predictable physics of magnetic dipole interactions.

If you have two magnetic dipoles (which can be generated using electromagnets or superconducting loops), they exert a force and a torque on one another based on their relative orientation, distance, and field strength.

The patent describes a system designed to:

Attract or Repel: Manipulate the magnetic fields to pull a target object closer or push it away.

Control Orientation: Adjust the torque to precisely rotate, stabilize, or align the target object in three-dimensional space without physical contact.

Dynamic Feedback: Continuously measure the position and orientation of the target, feeding that data into a control processor to adjust the magnetic fields in real-time, preventing the target from drifting or snapping violently out of position.

The Intended Application

In space, docking two multi-ton vehicles (like a shuttle or capsule connecting to a space station) is incredibly risky. Mechanical docking mechanisms require physical impact, which can transmit dangerous kinetic energy, cause structural fatigue, or send both craft into an uncontrolled tumble if misaligned.

The "Tractor Beam" Solution:

By using this magnetic dipole control system, a chaser spacecraft could:

Approach a passive target satellite or station module.

Activate the controlled magnetic fields from a safe distance.

Gently capture, damp out any residual spin (tumble) of the target, and guide it into a precise mechanical lock without ever touching it until the final capture.

It's a highly sophisticated, non-contact magnetic tethering and dampening system—essentially a high-tech "soft-capture" mechanism for orbital logistics.

#ufotwitter

It's fascinating milestone in the field of modern nanophotonics. Published in ACS Photonics in late 2018 by physicists from ITMO University, the research details how hyperbolic metasurfaces can be used to generate stable optical pulling forces—commonly referred to as an "optical tractor beam."

To understand how this functions, it helps to look at the mechanics of standard radiation pressure and contrast it with how these specialized, engineered surfaces flip that mechanic on its head.

The Problem with Normal Light

Under ordinary conditions, when a laser beam strikes a small dielectric particle, the photons transfer their linear momentum to the object. This creates a standard scattering or radiation pressure that pushes the particle away from the light source, along the direction of propagation.

Creating a "tractor beam" requires generating a negative optical force (a pulling force) that acts against the global momentum of the incoming light, drawing the particle backward toward the source.

How Hyperbolic Metasurfaces Change the Mechanics

A hyperbolic metasurface is an ultra-thin, artificially engineered material characterized by extreme anisotropy. This means its electromagnetic properties (specifically its permittivity, have different signs along different spatial axes—for example, acting like a metal  in one direction and like a dielectric in another.

When a small particle is placed close to this surface and illuminated by a standard unstructured light source (like a plane wave), the interaction relies on three core phenomena:

High Density of States & Asymmetric Scattering: The hyperbolic nature of the substrate opens up highly localized, high-density electromagnetic interaction channels (both surface plasmons and volumetric waves within the substrate). The presence of the particle scatterer near the interface selectively excites these asymmetrical near-field modes.

Momentum Conservation and Recoil: Because the substrate allows light to scatter preferentially into these highly confined, high-momentum modes directed forward along or into the surface, the law of conservation of linear momentum requires a balance. To compensate for the forward momentum of the tailored scattering channels, the particle experiences a net mechanical recoil in the opposite direction.

Broadband Pulling: Unlike older optical trapping configurations that require complex, actively modulated phase holograms or spatial light modulators (SLMs) to sculpt a specific beam geometry (like a solenoid or Bessel beam), this model utilizes the intrinsic dispersion of the substrate itself. This allows for stable optical pulling forces over a significantly broader spectral range using simpler incident light fields.

Why This Mattered

Prior to this approach, pulling particles with light required highly complex, bulky optical setups that restricted spatial movement and demanded high laser power, which could damage biological samples or nanoscale structures. By shifting the engineering challenge from the light source to the material environment (the metasurface), this framework opened up new avenues for nanoscale manipulation, chip-based sorting of cells or macromolecules, and nanoscale fluidic self-organization.

United States: Boeing's Magnetic Tractor Beam Patent

This is an interesting find. You’re looking at US Patent US6634603B2, titled "Magnetic dipole tractor beam control system", filed by The Boeing Company back in 2001 and granted in 2003.

While the phrase "tractor beam" instantly evokes sci-fi, the actual engineering framework here is grounded in classic electromagnetic field manipulation. Here is a breakdown of what this patent actually describes, how it works, and its intended application.

The Core Concept: How It Works

Unlike fictional tractor beams that use exotic energy to pull objects across massive distances, this system relies on the predictable physics of magnetic dipole interactions.

If you have two magnetic dipoles (which can be generated using electromagnets or superconducting loops), they exert a force and a torque on one another based on their relative orientation, distance, and field strength.

The patent describes a system designed to:

Attract or Repel: Manipulate the magnetic fields to pull a target object closer or push it away.

Control Orientation: Adjust the torque to precisely rotate, stabilize, or align the target object in three-dimensional space without physical contact.

Dynamic Feedback: Continuously measure the position and orientation of the target, feeding that data into a control processor to adjust the magnetic fields in real-time, preventing the target from drifting or snapping violently out of position.

The Intended Application

In space, docking two multi-ton vehicles (like a shuttle or capsule connecting to a space station) is incredibly risky. Mechanical docking mechanisms require physical impact, which can transmit dangerous kinetic energy, cause structural fatigue, or send both craft into an uncontrolled tumble if misaligned.

The "Tractor Beam" Solution:

By using this magnetic dipole control system, a chaser spacecraft could:

Approach a passive target satellite or station module.

Activate the controlled magnetic fields from a safe distance.

Gently capture, damp out any residual spin (tumble) of the target, and guide it into a precise mechanical lock without ever touching it until the final capture.

It's a highly sophisticated, non-contact magnetic tethering and dampening system—essentially a high-tech "soft-capture" mechanism for orbital logistics.

John Cole & Dr. Frank Mead: Both have histories connected to advanced or breakthrough propulsion concepts within institutional or military frameworks.

Professor at MEPhI: Specifically within the Department of Electrophysical Installations, which deals directly with high-energy systems, particle accelerators, and plasma physics.

🚨 A new AI infrastructure bottleneck just got its own ETF — backed by SemiAnalysis

Introducing $LAZR — the Tema Photonics & Optical ETF, launched in direct partnership with @SemiAnalysis, the leading independent research shop in semis & AI infra.

$LAZR is pure-play exposure to the optics layer moving data between chips, servers & data centers at AI scale — the second critical bottleneck after memory (its sister fund $DISK)

Top 10 holdings:

Lumentum ( $LITE) 14.6% — lasers/transceivers powering high-speed data center links

Cash  holdings 11.4%

Aixtron 10.5% — German MOCVD deposition equipment for compound semis

$AXTI AXT Inc 9.7% — substrates underpinning optical & power devices

$AAOI Applied Optoelectronics 5.8%

Furukawa Electric 5.2%

$TSEM Tower Semiconductor 4.1%

Landmark Optoelectronics ~3.9%

$AEHR Aehr Test Systems 3.5%

Yangtze Optical Fibre & Cable 3.4%

Why it matters?? 
📊Goldman projects the AI data-movement/photonics market to grow 9x between 2026-2028. 
📈As bandwidth needs outrun copper’s physics, silicon photonics stops being a niche play and becomes core infrastructure.

text:
Vergiss das 1. Halbjahr – ab jetzt wird neu gemischt! 📉📈

Statt auf den nächsten unprofitablen Hype zu hoffen, fokussiere ich mich auf diese 3 Kandidaten:
 $MSFT – bleibt das unzerstörbare Fundament der weltweiten Software-Infrastruktur.
$PLTR – beweist immer deutlicher, wie man mit KI-gestützter Daten-Analyse in der echten Wirtschaft massiv Geld verdient.
$SIVE – Sivers Semiconductors liefert die entscheidende Photonics-Lasertechnologie für KI-Rechenzentren. Die Volatilität nach der jüngsten Kapitalerhöhung bietet jetzt eine brutal spannende Einstiegschance.

Setzt ihr im 2. Halbjahr nur auf die bekannten Tech-Giganten oder habt ihr auch solche Small-Cap-Wetten im Depot?

text:
Daily Buzzberg Alpha — July 3

The tape sold AI/semis like Meta capex = overcapacity. The best sources read the same data as proof of scarcity.

1. The neocloud selloff looks like a misread. SemiAnalysis flipped the bear narrative: Meta isn't cutting AI capex — it's turning compute into a business. 5GW+ already contracted in H1, and 2027 capex could come in above expectations. Short-term compute reportedly trades ~4x long-term neocloud rates — that's not what surplus looks like. Read-through: $NBIS $CRWV $IREN $CIFR. The outlier: $ORCL, flagged for weak compute monetization vs peers.

2. Memory upgraded from "shortage" to pricing power. The HBM chatter got specific: 2027 contract talk now includes 2–2.5x price increases vs broker estimates. CNBC: the Apple AI iPhone cycle could lift memory demand ~50%, with Apple hunting supply even from Chinese makers. Note — the same Apple-China headline traded as a bear signal yesterday; today's read is demand outrunning supply. Macro counterpoint: pricing near peak, trade crowded. $MU  $SNDK , Samsung and SK Hynix in Seoul.

3. Korea is a market-structure trade now, not just a memory proxy. Local channels near-unanimous: the selloff was technical — 2x leveraged products, foreign flows, pension rebalancing — not broken fundamentals. Earnings + HBM LTAs + cheap valuation on one side, index-shock sensitivity to every AI rumor on the other. $EWY.

4. Photonics/packaging — best undercovered signal of the day. From SemiAnalysis' ECTC notes: Marvell custom HBM cuts host ASIC area ~60%, TSMC direct-to-silicon cooling handles >5kW, optical interconnects becoming the core scaling path. Supply-chain angle: $LITE demand reportedly 30%+ ahead of supply, $COHR locking wafer supply via $AXTI, $CIEN as the cleaner transport play. Also $MRVL $IQE $TSM $INTC.

5. Physical AI: quieter, higher quality. BlueCrest disclosed a 5.6% stake in $CCXI  (the Agility Robotics proxy). $ONDS expanded its counter-drone TAM from federal to state/local. $BOT as the public basket. $OUST cooling off on fresh mentions.

The non-consensus read: if compute is still scarce and Meta is monetizing it, yesterday's selloff was positioning and liquidity — not a fundamental break.

text:
WHILE EVERYONE PANICS OVER CHIP STOCKS, THE REAL AI INFRASTRUCTURE PLAY IS QUIETLY SELLING OFF TOO.

$AMKR Amkor Technology down 12.93%. Semiconductor packaging and testing, critical link between chip design and the end product.

$LITE Lumentum down 9.09%. Optical components and photonics for datacenters and telecom, key infrastructure for AI networking.

$VRT Vertiv down 3.5%. Power and cooling infrastructure for data centers, the backbone that keeps AI compute running.

$MPWR Monolithic Power Systems down 3.27%. Power management chips used across AI hardware.

Different layers of the same AI buildout story, all getting hit together.

DYOR.

text:
Posted by Antithesis Capital on LinkedIn in February 2026: 

Capitalizing on the Semiconductor Demand Cycle on the OTC market

SCI Engineered Materials (SCIA) is a global supplier and manufacturer of advanced materials for Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) thin film applications. SCIA is mainly focused on markets within the photonics industry. Their main product is Sputtering Targets which are specialized solid materials used in thin-film deposition processes called Sputtering for semiconductors, displays, optics, and coatings. 

How Sputtering Works:
- A sputtering target acts as the source material
- The target is bombarded with high-energy ions 
- These ions knock atoms or molecules loose from the target's surface
- The freed atoms travel through the vacuum and deposit onto a nearby surface (the substrate), forming an extremely thin, uniform film 

Demand for sputtering targets continues to increase as the Semiconductor boom accelerates. Sputtering targets are  essential for depositing thin-film layers (interconnects like copper, barriers like tantalum/tungsten, insulators) during wafer fabrication via physical vapor deposition.

SCIA trades on the OTC market with a market-cap of $26M. Meanwhile, their balance sheet consists of the following: 

Cash: $9.5M
A/R: $900K
Inventory: $3M 
Marketable Securities: $3.3M
Total Liabilities: $6.3M 

$4M of the $6.3M in Total Liabilities is Customer Deposits which can be better defined as Order Backlog and signals on-going demand for SCIA’s products. SCIA had $1.9M and $2.2M in Net Income in 2024 and 2023 respectively. They’ve made $1.2M in Net Income through 9/30/2025. 

SCIA’s closest NYSE/NASDAQ comp is Materion Corporation (MTRN). Materion is an integrated producer of high-performance advanced engineered materials used in a variety of electrical, thermal, and structural applications. 

MRTN trades at an above market multiple with a PE of 163. In addition, they were recently upgraded to Overweight by KeyBank. According to the report, data points suggest MTRN is well positioned to capitalize on a global semiconductor consumable demand cycle which could feature higher aerospace and defense budgets, expanding clean energy businesses, and opportunities for margin expansion. 

We agree with KeyBank’s thought process and think SCI Engineered Materials will have a chance to capitalize on this same demand cycle. In our opinion, SCIA is a much more attractive way to play this demand cycle thesis due their rock solid balance sheet and reasonable PE multiple (16x). 

We are not the only ones who think this. Management, on 11/17/25, announced a $1 million share repurchase authorization. Clearly, they see the same value. 

SCIA is set to report FY25 earnings this week.

"id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzMxMjAzODQwNTMwODQxNjA=", "text": "Posted by Antithesis Capital on LinkedIn in February 2026: Capitalizing on the Semiconductor Demand Cycle on the OTC market SCI Engineered Materials (SCIA) is a global supplier and manufacturer of advanced materials for Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) thin film applications. SCIA is mainly focused on markets within the photonics industry. Their main product is Sputtering Targets which are specialized solid materials used in thin-film deposition processes called Sputtering for semiconductors, displays, optics, and coatings. How Sputtering Works: - A sputtering target acts as the source material - The target is bombarded with high-energy ions - These ions knock atoms or molecules loose from the target's surface - The freed atoms travel through the vacuum and deposit onto a nearby surface (the substrate), forming an extremely thin, uniform film Demand for sputtering targets continues to increase as the Semiconductor boom accelerates. Sputtering targets are essential for depositing thin-film layers (interconnects like copper, barriers like tantalum/tungsten, insulators) during wafer fabrication via physical vapor deposition. SCIA trades on the OTC market with a market-cap of $26M. Meanwhile, their balance sheet consists of the following: Cash: $9.5M A/R: $900K Inventory: $3M Marketable Securities: $3.3M Total Liabilities: $6.3M $4M of the $6.3M in Total Liabilities is Customer Deposits which can be better defined as Order Backlog and signals on-going demand for SCIA’s products. SCIA had $1.9M and $2.2M in Net Income in 2024 and 2023 respectively. They’ve made $1.2M in Net Income through 9/30/2025. SCIA’s closest NYSE/NASDAQ comp is Materion Corporation (MTRN). Materion is an integrated producer of high-performance advanced engineered materials used in a variety of electrical, thermal, and structural applications. MRTN trades at an above market multiple with a PE of 163. In addition, they were recently upgraded to Overweight by KeyBank. According to the report, data points suggest MTRN is well positioned to capitalize on a global semiconductor consumable demand cycle which could feature higher aerospace and defense budgets, expanding clean energy businesses, and opportunities for margin expansion. We agree with KeyBank’s thought process and think SCI Engineered Materials will have a chance to capitalize on this same demand cycle. In our opinion, SCIA is a much more attractive way to play this demand cycle thesis due their rock solid balance sheet and reasonable PE multiple (16x). We are not the only ones who think this. Management, on 11/17/25, announced a $1 million share repurchase authorization. Clearly, they see the same value. SCIA is set to report FY25 earnings this week."}}}, "rest_id": "2073120384283807751", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "39", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The firm whose bearish CPO paper helped tank optical stocks just launched a photonics ETF and it's holding $SIVE.

Walk through what actually happened:

→ SemiAnalysis published a scathing piece on CPO delays and optical valuations. Optical names sold off 40-60%.

→ On June 30, SemiAnalysis co-launched the Tema Photonics & Optical ETF (NYSE: LAZR) — the first institutionally managed photonics ETF — in partnership with Tema ETFs.

→ As of July 1, that ETF holds 26,688 shares of $SIVE, bought around 48 SEK.

So the same research shop that helped drive the sector down is now buying into it through an institutional product — a 24-name concentrated fund built specifically for photonics and optical exposure.

I'm not going to call that anything it isn't. What I'll say is this:

When you get to publish the bearish note AND launch the fund that buys the dip, that's the most enviable seat in the market. And the name they put in the fund — out of every photonics company on Earth, in a concentrated 24-holding portfolio — was $SIVE.

An actively managed, SemiAnalysis-backed, institutional photonics fund looked at the entire optical universe and decided Sivers belonged in the 24.

That's not retail hype. That's a research-driven institutional desk putting $SIVE in the same basket as Lumentum, Coherent, and AXT.

The company keeps ending up exactly where the smartest money in the sector is looking.

$SIVE $LAZR

text:
Here's the takeaway that actually matters: $AAOI, $LITE, $COHR, $CIEN, $NOK are down 25-55% on sector weakness.

The photonics demand curve extends into 2027-2028 regardless of when CPO hits mass production. As clusters scale, copper hits a wall. Optical isn't optional. I'm buying the fear, not the narrative.

How far off their highs, and what you make just reclaiming them:

• $SIVEF $5.24 | down 53% | +115% to reclaim $11.25
• $AAOI $121 | down 48% | +93% to reclaim $234
• $CIEN $422 | down 34% | +51% to reclaim $638
• $LITE $725 | down 33% | +50% to reclaim $1,086
• $NOK $12 | down 31% | +45% to reclaim $17.45 (52w)
• $COHR $333 | down 24% | +32% to reclaim $440

And that's just to tag old highs.

text:
AXT เซ็นดีล 3 ปีกับ Coherent ขยายกำลังผลิตวัสดุชิปโฟโตนิกส์

AXT $AXTI เปิดเผยผ่านเอกสารยื่นต่อ SEC ว่าบริษัทในเครือ AXT-Tongmei ได้ทำข้อตกลงระยะเวลา 3 ปีร่วมกับ Coherent $COHR

ดีลนี้เกี่ยวข้องกับการพัฒนาและจัดส่ง substrate อินเดียมฟอสไฟด์ขนาด 6 นิ้ว ซึ่งเป็นวัสดุสำคัญในอุตสาหกรรม photonics หรือเทคโนโลยีการส่งข้อมูลด้วยแสง

ขยายกำลังผลิตในจีนเพื่อรองรับคำสั่งซื้อ
ภายใต้ข้อตกลงนี้ AXT จะขยายกำลังการผลิตที่โรงงานในกรุงปักกิ่ง ระหว่างปี 2026 ถึง 2028
โดยผลผลิตทั้งหมดจะถูกจัดสรรให้กับ Coherent ตามสัญญาที่กำหนดไว้

Coherent วางเงินล่วงหน้ากว่า 22 ล้านดอลลาร์
Coherent จะจ่ายเงินล่วงหน้าประมาณ 22.29 ล้านดอลลาร์สหรัฐ
เงินจำนวนนี้จะถูกนำไปใช้เป็นเครดิตสำหรับการซื้อสินค้าล็อตในอนาคต

อย่างไรก็ตาม เอกสารระบุเงื่อนไขสำคัญว่า
หาก Coherent ใช้คำสั่งซื้อตามขั้นต่ำไม่ครบ เงินล่วงหน้าอาจ “ไม่สามารถขอคืนได้”
แต่หาก Coherent เลือกยกเลิกดีลก่อนโดยไม่ผิดเงื่อนไข จะสามารถขอคืนเงินได้

ในทางกลับกัน
ถ้า AXT ส่งมอบกำลังผลิตไม่ได้ตามที่ตกลงนานเกิน 6 เดือน
Coherent มีสิทธิ์ยกเลิกสัญญาและขอคืนเงินที่ยังไม่ได้ใช้

text:
$AXTI $COHR Do you believe this is last InP prepayment deal that will be cut over the next 5 years? We merely getting started.
———————-
BOTTOM LINE: This is incrementally bullish for COHR fundamentals but more strategically important than financially material. It confirms that 6-inch InP substrate supply remains a gating item for COHR’s AI optical ramp, validates AXT/Tongmei as a meaningful 6-inch InP substrate supplier, and shows COHR is using customer-style capacity prepayments upstream to lock supply through the next 3 years. It should be more stock-moving for AXTI than COHR, but for COHR it is a positive de-risking datapoint after a weak tape day, not a thesis-changing event by itself.

The agreement is straightforward but important. AXT-Tongmei entered a 3-year Master Development and Supply Agreement with Coherent, effective June 25, 2026, covering “mass development and supply” of 6-inch indium phosphide wafer substrates. AXT will expand capacity at its Beijing facility from 2026 through 2028, Coherent will make a $22.2885M prepayment for committed capacity, wafer prices are pre-established, and the prepayment will be applied against future product purchases. Coherent also gets refund rights if unused amounts remain at expiry or termination, and can terminate and recover unused prepayment if AXT fails to meet the capacity commitment for more than 6 successive months. AXT gets protection if Coherent misses minimum order quantities, in which case unused prepayment becomes nonrefundable and AXT can terminate. AXT must also offer additional capacity beyond the committed quantity to Coherent on the same terms. The full agreement will not be available until AXT files its Q2 2026 10-Q.  

The key interpretation is that this is not about outsourcing COHR’s differentiated InP device manufacturing. AXT is supplying 6-inch InP wafer substrates, the upstream crystalline substrate input. Coherent still does the value-added photonic device work: EMLs, CW lasers, photodiodes, PICs and related integration. That distinction matters because COHR’s moat remains process/yield/device manufacturing and vertical integration; AXT is enabling more wafer starts, not replacing COHR’s 6-inch InP fab advantage.

For COHR, the positive read is supply-chain de-risking. Management has repeatedly framed InP capacity as the key constraint in the AI optical cycle, while also saying 6-inch InP is central to the revenue and gross margin ramp. In the May earnings call, COHR said its 6-inch platform was producing EMLs, CW lasers and photodiodes, with yields exceeding 3-inch lines, first 6-inch-derived transceiver shipments already contributing to revenue and margin, and internal InP capacity expected to double ahead of plan and more than double again by the end of calendar 2027.   This AXT agreement strengthens confidence that substrate availability will not become the hidden bottleneck that prevents those internal wafer starts from scaling.

This also lines up with the J.P. Morgan investor-meeting takeaway that COHR had substrate supply “locked in” across 5 suppliers spanning multiple geographies, several capable of producing 6-inch substrates. The AXT filing effectively names 1 of those upstream supply arrangements and shows there is real money, fixed pricing, and committed capacity behind the company’s prior substrate comments.  

The less bullish read is that the deal confirms the bottleneck is real and persistent. Coherent would not be making take-or-pay-like commitments and prepaying a supplier for 3-year capacity if 6-inch InP substrates were abundant. Reuters recently reported that China’s InP export controls have disrupted the AI photonics supply chain, that AXT and Sumitomo dominate global InP substrate production, that AXT is a major supplier to Coherent, and that 6-inch InP wafer prices have reportedly risen sharply since China tightened export licensing.   The agreement helps COHR, but it does not eliminate geopolitical risk because the capacity expansion is at AXT’s Beijing facility, precisely the jurisdiction where export licensing and materials controls have become a live issue.

The deal is financially immaterial to COHR in isolation. COHR’s market cap is roughly $65B and the $22.3M prepayment is de minimis against the equity value, capex program, and cash balance. It is best viewed as a capacity reservation / working-capital deposit, likely recorded as a prepaid asset and then consumed through substrate purchases, not as an immediate P&L event. COHR was trading near $333 after a roughly -9.6% move on July 2, so the stock reaction is more likely to be driven by AI hardware sentiment, valuation, and semiconductor tape than by a $22M supplier prepayment.

For AXTI, however, the signal is materially stronger. AXT reported Q1 2026 revenue of $26.9M, so a $22.3M prepayment is roughly 83% of a single quarter’s reported revenue, even though it is not immediately recognized as revenue and the total contract value could be meaningfully different from the prepayment. AXT also said in April that it had completed a $632.5M capital raise to support Tongmei’s InP capacity expansion and 6-inch InP R&D, so the COHR contract provides named strategic-customer validation for that capital deployment.   AXTI was down roughly -12.9% on July 2, and this after-close filing could support a rebound when regular trading resumes.

The most important subtlety is that the $22.3M should not be treated as the total revenue opportunity. The filing says the prepayment is applied against purchases and ties to committed capacity; it does not disclose wafer volumes, per-wafer prices, shipment cadence, gross margin, or whether additional capacity is expected to be purchased beyond the initial commitment. Using Reuters’ cited $5,000 average 6-inch InP wafer price as a purely illustrative benchmark, the prepayment would equate to roughly 4,500 wafers, but that calculation should not be used as a volume estimate because actual contract pricing is confidential and the prepayment may only represent a portion of the broader commitment.  

The fixed-price element is meaningful. If spot 6-inch InP pricing continues to rise, Coherent has partially hedged substrate cost and capacity access. That matters for the gross margin bridge because COHR’s >42% non-GAAP gross margin path relies on 6-inch cost/yield benefits, higher 1.6T mix, internal sourcing, and better absorption. J.P. Morgan’s May conference note highlighted 42%+ gross margin tailwinds from the shift from 800G to higher-margin 1.6T, the transition from 3-inch to 6-inch wafers, and movement from external to internal sourcing.   The AXT agreement supports that framework, but the benefit likely shows up gradually through wafer starts, device output, module shipments, and margin conversion rather than immediately in the next quarter.

This is also a useful read-through to AI optical demand. Coherent is not just receiving long-term customer commitments; it is passing those commitments upstream. That is how a real supply-constrained hardware cycle behaves. Customers commit to COHR, COHR commits to AXT, AXT expands Beijing 6-inch capacity, and the entire chain becomes increasingly governed by deposits, capacity reservations, fixed prices, minimum orders, and termination protections. This reinforces the view that the AI optical cycle is capacity constrained rather than purely speculative order inflation, though it does not disprove some double-ordering risk.

The agreement also has a China-risk wrinkle that investors should not ignore. Coherent has emphasized U.S. manufacturing scale, Sherman 6-inch InP leadership, CHIPS-related support, and the NVIDIA strategic partnership. This AXT agreement reveals that even a U.S.-anchored 6-inch InP device strategy still depends on upstream substrate inputs from China. That is not inherently negative, because AXT is a scaled and strategically important substrate supplier, but it means export permits, U.S.-China policy, force majeure language, and qualification of non-China supply remain critical diligence items.

Relative implications are clear. For COHR, this is a positive data point for execution confidence and supply visibility, but not enough on its own to revise estimates. For AXTI, it is a much bigger validation event because it confirms that a leading AI optical platform company is willing to prepay for Tongmei 6-inch substrate capacity. For LITE, the read-through is mixed: positive for the industry because InP scarcity is validated, but potentially negative at the margin if COHR is locking a larger share of scarce AXT 6-inch output. For the broader optical ecosystem, the deal supports the view that the scarcity value sits not only in finished transceivers but also deep upstream in compound semiconductor substrates, lasers, isolators, PM fiber, ELS modules, and other enabling components.

Trading implication: this should help stabilize the COHR narrative after the July 2 selloff, but it is not large enough to single-handedly re-rate the stock. The better near-term setup is probably AXTI up more sharply on customer validation and COHR modestly supported, with the caveat that U.S. markets are closed Friday, July 3 for Independence Day observed, so regular-session price discovery is delayed.   COHR remains a high-conviction structural AI optical beneficiary, but the stock still needs gross margin progression, 6-inch output conversion, and FCF inflection to justify the valuation. This announcement improves confidence in the supply-chain bridge to that outcome.

Key diligence questions for Monday/next week: whether this AXT capacity was already included in COHR’s “5 substrate suppliers” commentary or is incremental; whether export licenses are already secured; what share of FY2027 6-inch wafer starts this covers; whether the agreement includes first-priority capacity rights or merely committed capacity; whether the fixed wafer prices are below current spot; how quickly AXT can qualify volume 6-inch wafers to COHR specifications; and whether any of the prepayment is ultimately customer-funded through COHR’s own strategic LTAs.

AXT will expand capacity at its Beijing facility from 2026 through 2028, Coherent will make a $22.2885M prepayment for committed capacity, wafer prices are pre-established, and the prepayment will be applied against future product purchases. Coherent also gets refund rights if unused amounts remain at expiry or termination, and can terminate and recover unused prepayment if AXT fails to meet the capacity commitment for more than 6 successive months. AXT gets protection if Coherent misses minimum order quantities, in which case unused prepayment becomes nonrefundable and AXT can terminate. AXT must also offer additional capacity beyond the committed quantity to Coherent on the same terms. The full agreement will not be available until AXT files its Q2 2026 10-Q. The key interpretation is that this is not about outsourcing COHR’s differentiated InP device manufacturing. AXT is supplying 6-inch InP wafer substrates, the upstream crystalline substrate input. Coherent still does the value-added photonic device work: EMLs, CW lasers, photodiodes, PICs and related integration. That distinction matters because COHR’s moat remains process/yield/device manufacturing and vertical integration; AXT is enabling more wafer starts, not replacing COHR’s 6-inch InP fab advantage. For COHR, the positive read is supply-chain de-risking. Management has repeatedly framed InP capacity as the key constraint in the AI optical cycle, while also saying 6-inch InP is central to the revenue and gross margin ramp. In the May earnings call, COHR said its 6-inch platform was producing EMLs, CW lasers and photodiodes, with yields exceeding 3-inch lines, first 6-inch-derived transceiver shipments already contributing to revenue and margin, and internal InP capacity expected to double ahead of plan and more than double again by the end of calendar 2027. This AXT agreement strengthens confidence that substrate availability will not become the hidden bottleneck that prevents those internal wafer starts from scaling. This also lines up with the J.P. Morgan investor-meeting takeaway that COHR had substrate supply “locked in” across 5 suppliers spanning multiple geographies, several capable of producing 6-inch substrates. The AXT filing effectively names 1 of those upstream supply arrangements and shows there is real money, fixed pricing, and committed capacity behind the company’s prior substrate comments. The less bullish read is that the deal confirms the bottleneck is real and persistent. Coherent would not be making take-or-pay-like commitments and prepaying a supplier for 3-year capacity if 6-inch InP substrates were abundant. Reuters recently reported that China’s InP export controls have disrupted the AI photonics supply chain, that AXT and Sumitomo dominate global InP substrate production, that AXT is a major supplier to Coherent, and that 6-inch InP wafer prices have reportedly risen sharply since China tightened export licensing. The agreement helps COHR, but it does not eliminate geopolitical risk because the capacity expansion is at AXT’s Beijing facility, precisely the jurisdiction where export licensing and materials controls have become a live issue. The deal is financially immaterial to COHR in isolation. COHR’s market cap is roughly $65B and the $22.3M prepayment is de minimis against the equity value, capex program, and cash balance. It is best viewed as a capacity reservation / working-capital deposit, likely recorded as a prepaid asset and then consumed through substrate purchases, not as an immediate P&L event. COHR was trading near $333 after a roughly -9.6% move on July 2, so the stock reaction is more likely to be driven by AI hardware sentiment, valuation, and semiconductor tape than by a $22M supplier prepayment. For AXTI, however, the signal is materially stronger. AXT reported Q1 2026 revenue of $26.9M, so a $22.3M prepayment is roughly 83% of a single quarter’s reported revenue, even though it is not immediately recognized as revenue and the total contract value could be meaningfully different from the prepayment. AXT also said in April that it had completed a $632.5M capital raise to support Tongmei’s InP capacity expansion and 6-inch InP R&D, so the COHR contract provides named strategic-customer validation for that capital deployment. AXTI was down roughly -12.9% on July 2, and this after-close filing could support a rebound when regular trading resumes. The most important subtlety is that the $22.3M should not be treated as the total revenue opportunity. The filing says the prepayment is applied against purchases and ties to committed capacity; it does not disclose wafer volumes, per-wafer prices, shipment cadence, gross margin, or whether additional capacity is expected to be purchased beyond the initial commitment. Using Reuters’ cited $5,000 average 6-inch InP wafer price as a purely illustrative benchmark, the prepayment would equate to roughly 4,500 wafers, but that calculation should not be used as a volume estimate because actual contract pricing is confidential and the prepayment may only represent a portion of the broader commitment. The fixed-price element is meaningful. If spot 6-inch InP pricing continues to rise, Coherent has partially hedged substrate cost and capacity access. That matters for the gross margin bridge because COHR’s >42% non-GAAP gross margin path relies on 6-inch cost/yield benefits, higher 1.6T mix, internal sourcing, and better absorption. J.P. Morgan’s May conference note highlighted 42%+ gross margin tailwinds from the shift from 800G to higher-margin 1.6T, the transition from 3-inch to 6-inch wafers, and movement from external to internal sourcing. The AXT agreement supports that framework, but the benefit likely shows up gradually through wafer starts, device output, module shipments, and margin conversion rather than immediately in the next quarter. This is also a useful read-through to AI optical demand. Coherent is not just receiving long-term customer commitments; it is passing those commitments upstream. That is how a real supply-constrained hardware cycle behaves. Customers commit to COHR, COHR commits to AXT, AXT expands Beijing 6-inch capacity, and the entire chain becomes increasingly governed by deposits, capacity reservations, fixed prices, minimum orders, and termination protections. This reinforces the view that the AI optical cycle is capacity constrained rather than purely speculative order inflation, though it does not disprove some double-ordering risk. The agreement also has a China-risk wrinkle that investors should not ignore. Coherent has emphasized U.S. manufacturing scale, Sherman 6-inch InP leadership, CHIPS-related support, and the NVIDIA strategic partnership. This AXT agreement reveals that even a U.S.-anchored 6-inch InP device strategy still depends on upstream substrate inputs from China. That is not inherently negative, because AXT is a scaled and strategically important substrate supplier, but it means export permits, U.S.-China policy, force majeure language, and qualification of non-China supply remain critical diligence items. Relative implications are clear. For COHR, this is a positive data point for execution confidence and supply visibility, but not enough on its own to revise estimates. For AXTI, it is a much bigger validation event because it confirms that a leading AI optical platform company is willing to prepay for Tongmei 6-inch substrate capacity. For LITE, the read-through is mixed: positive for the industry because InP scarcity is validated, but potentially negative at the margin if COHR is locking a larger share of scarce AXT 6-inch output. For the broader optical ecosystem, the deal supports the view that the scarcity value sits not only in finished transceivers but also deep upstream in compound semiconductor substrates, lasers, isolators, PM fiber, ELS modules, and other enabling components. Trading implication: this should help stabilize the COHR narrative after the July 2 selloff, but it is not large enough to single-handedly re-rate the stock. The better near-term setup is probably AXTI up more sharply on customer validation and COHR modestly supported, with the caveat that U.S. markets are closed Friday, July 3 for Independence Day observed, so regular-session price discovery is delayed. COHR remains a high-conviction structural AI optical beneficiary, but the stock still needs gross margin progression, 6-inch output conversion, and FCF inflection to justify the valuation. This announcement improves confidence in the supply-chain bridge to that outcome. Key diligence questions for Monday/next week: whether this AXT capacity was already included in COHR’s “5 substrate suppliers” commentary or is incremental; whether export licenses are already secured; what share of FY2027 6-inch wafer starts this covers; whether the agreement includes first-priority capacity rights or merely committed capacity; whether the fixed wafer prices are below current spot; how quickly AXT can qualify volume 6-inch wafers to COHR specifications; and whether any of the prepayment is ultimately customer-funded through COHR’s own strategic LTAs."}}}, "quoted_status_result": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "core": {"user_results": {"result": {"__typename": "User", "affiliates_highlighted_label": {}, "avatar": {"image_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1996176688414367744/LXfA_lIx_normal.jpg"}, "core": {"created_at": "Wed Jul 02 10:44:15 +0000 2025", "name": "Serenity", "screen_name": "aleabitoreddit"}, "dm_permissions": {"can_dm": true}, "follow_request_sent": false, "has_graduated_access": true, "id": "VXNlcjoxOTQwMzYwODM3NTQ3NTY1MDU2", "is_blue_verified": true, "legacy": {"default_profile": true, "default_profile_image": false, "description": "Only on X, don’t trust fake accs AI/Semi Supply Chains NFA DYOR, no paid promos; may trade/hold names disc, views my own. text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — July 2, 2026

📊 SMH: $592.29 (-4.54%)

📈 Earnings:
• No new semiconductor earnings released today.

🔥 Major Developments:
• AXT-Tongmei signed a three-year 6-inch InP wafer substrate capacity agreement with Coherent, including a $22.3M prepayment tied to expanded Beijing capacity from 2026-2028. The optics supply chain is moving from spot-market scramble to locked capacity, which matters for Coherent, AXT, and the broader AI photonics bottleneck.
• Anthropic is exploring a custom AI chip with Samsung as a potential manufacturing partner, evaluating Samsung’s 2nm process and advanced packaging. If it moves forward, the read-through is bigger than one AI lab: frontier model companies are trying to control silicon roadmaps, and Samsung is still hunting for a marquee AI foundry win against TSMC.
• Nvidia is offering smaller GPU cloud providers financial backstops and revenue-share structures tied to unused GPU capacity. That supports near-term accelerator demand, but it also makes the AI infrastructure stack more circular: chip demand, cloud financing, and end-customer utilization are becoming increasingly intertwined.
• Meta said AI agent development over the last four months has not accelerated as expected, adding pressure to the capex ROI debate after reports that it may sell excess AI compute capacity. The takeaway is not that AI demand is dead; it is that hyperscaler capex now needs cleaner monetization proof, not just bigger buildout numbers.

text:
Hello darkness my old friend. 

Right when I think we’re done, you find a way to lure me back in!

I made peace with cutting $AXTI yesterday and it somehow finds a way to drag me back in. 

This deal is huge and the pre payment alone is nearly as much as their entire Q1 revenue. 

Not only that but it’s the catalyst it needs to shake off the recent fear around the AI infra trade and specifically photonics. 

This is my fifth time trying to trade AXTI in the past month. Is this time different?

text:
Fiber & Infrastructure Highlights – July 2, 2026

Conexon Connect Georgia completed its ninth FTTH project with Satilla REMC, surpassing 240,000 homes/businesses, 5,200 fiber miles, and delivering multi-gig to ~60,000 locations across 9 counties. Vocus announced ~A$500M ducted Sydney-Melbourne long-haul (6,912 cores, ready 2029, AI-driven). Cloud Capital/Realty Income formed a $6B data center JV (Virginia). South Korea outlined $919B mega projects with 18.4GW data centers by 2035. Nixxy/Tachyon announced a 1GW North Dakota campus. Tata advanced India-Singapore subsea.

Ongoing AI hyperscaler demand drives fiber supply ramps (Corning, STL, CommScope, Clearfield, Fujikura tripling production) amid persistent shortages (preforms tight through 2026+). Regional OSP/ISP activity includes Ziply Northwest, Ripple Fiber WA, Ezee Fiber, Brightspeed WiFi 7, Burlington Telecom 5 Gbps, Heartland Fiber Project, FiberLight West Texas, Big Fiber financing, Atomic Fiber Los Alamos, and various BEAD/FTTH builds. Manufacturing focuses on high-count, multi-core, photonics; FBA whitepaper on fiber-AI convergence and power/grid constraints add momentum.

 #FiberForge #FiberOptics #OSPISP

text:
【量子ハードウェア】
Pasqalがカナダで光集積回路（PIC）実装拠点を新設。790万ドル（約11.9億円、1ドル=150円換算）規模で量子・センシング向け製造基盤を強化。
（2026年7月3日 日本時間、Quantum Computing Report）

何が起きたか：
Pasqalは、買収したカナダ子会社Aeponyxを通じて、ケベック州ブロモンのC2MI（MiQro Innovation Collaborative Center）内に、光集積回路（PIC）パッケージングの専門拠点を設立すると発表した。 

このプロジェクトは総額790万ドル（約11.9億円、1ドル=150円換算）で、うち400万ドル（約6.0億円）は連邦・州政府が支援し、その中にはNGen（Next Generation Manufacturing Canada）からの300万ドル（約4.5億円）が含まれる。 

拠点では、シリコンナイトライド（SiN）フォトニクスを活用したPICの実装・組立ラインを構築し、レーザー制御モジュールなど量子コンピュータや量子センシングに必要な光部品の量産体制を整える。初期は試作・少量生産を担い、将来的には年間50万個超のモジュール生産能力を目指す計画だ。HOP Technologies、Phantom Photonicsも参画し、医療センシングやLiDARなど量子以外の用途も視野に入れる。 

読み解き：
今回のポイントは「量子コンピュータを作る」ことではなく、「量子コンピュータを量産できる部品産業を作る」ことにある。

量子コンピュータではチップそのもの以上に、レーザー、光導波路、ファイバー接続などの光学実装が製造ボトルネックになりやすい。特に中性原子方式は多数のレーザーを高精度で制御する必要があり、PICの高品質な実装技術は性能だけでなく生産性にも直結する。 

興味深いのは、この拠点が量子専用工場ではなく、防衛向けLiDARや医療センシング企業も巻き込んだ共通インフラとして設計されている点だ。同じフォトニクス製造設備を複数産業で共有できれば、量子産業単独では難しい設備投資の採算性を高められる。近年は各国が「量子主権」を意識したサプライチェーン整備を進めているが、この発表も製造能力そのものを国内に確保する流れの一例と言える。Pasqalにとっても、Aeponyx買収後の垂直統合を具体的な製造基盤へ発展させる動きとして位置付けられる。 

評価：
・業界インパクト：★★★
・注目度：★★★
・関連銘柄：PASQAL（未上場）

結論：
量子業界ではプロセッサ性能だけでなく、「安定して作れる製造基盤」が競争力になり始めている。今後は各社が光部品やパッケージング、製造拠点への投資をどこまで拡大するかが、商用展開の速度を左右する重要な指標になりそうだ。

text:
【研究開発】
米NSFが量子フォトニクス実用化へ400万ドル追加支援。GPS不要の量子ナビゲーションを視野に、最終段階では最大5,500万ドル規模の量子センター構想へ発展する可能性。
（2026年7月3日 〔日本時間〕・The Quantum Insider）

何が起きたか：
米ミシガン大学が主導する量子フォトニクス研究プロジェクト「QuPID（Quantum Photonic Integration and Deployment）」が、米国立科学財団（NSF）のNational Quantum Virtual Laboratory（NQVL）プログラムのフェーズ2に採択され、2年間で400万ドル（約6億円、1ドル=150円換算）の助成を受けることになった。これはフェーズ1の100万ドル（約1.5億円）に続く資金で、将来的には最大5,500万ドル（約82.5億円）のNSF量子センターへ発展する可能性がある。研究では、量子状態の光を利用した集積フォトニクスチップを開発し、GPSが使えない水中・宇宙・月面などでも利用できる高精度ナビゲーションや超高精度センシングを目指す。プロジェクトにはオハイオ州立大学、ハーバード大学、スタンフォード大学、パデュー大学のほか、Honeywell、TOPTICA Photonics、General Motors、Toyota、Raytheon、NASA Glenn Research Center、Air Force Research Laboratoryなど産学官が参加している。 

読み解き：
このニュースの本質は、「量子コンピュータ」ではなく「量子フォトニクス」の商用化に研究資金の重心が移っている点にある。今回の目標は演算性能ではなく、量子光学を使った実環境向けセンサーやナビゲーションであり、量子技術をインフラへ組み込む流れを象徴している。

特にNSFがフェーズ制で資金を拡大し、最終的には数千万ドル規模の量子センターを構想していることは、単なる大学研究ではなく、量産・実装を前提としたエコシステム形成を狙っていることを示す。さらに、自動車メーカー、防衛企業、NASAまで参加していることから、出口市場が明確な研究テーマとして位置付けられている点も重要だ。

量子業界では計算機だけでなく、量子センシングや量子通信が商用化を先行する可能性が高いと見られており、今回の支援はその流れを裏付ける動きといえる。 

評価：
・業界インパクト：★★★
・注目度：★★★
・関連銘柄：HON, GM, TM

結論：
量子技術の競争軸は計算能力だけではなく、センシングやナビゲーションなど現実世界の用途へ広がりつつある。今後は、このような研究プロジェクトが実証実験や政府調達へ発展するかどうかが、商用化を見極める重要なポイントになる。

text:
The Market That Changed Engines Mid‑Flight Strategic & Tactical Read of the Day 

Today's tape told a story that wasn't subtle: the market didn't break, it pivoted. Momentum the great engine of the 2025–H1 2026 rally finally lost altitude. Semiconductors, memory, photonics, and the AI‑power complex all slipped into a decisive unwind. META admitted AI progress has been slower than expected; photonics rejected their 21‑DMA; semis logged a second straight 5%+ selloff; MTUM printed its sixth rare >3% down day while RSP rose. That's not noise that's regime change.

Yet beneath the surface, the bull market kept breathing. Defensive sectors surged: healthcare, staples, utilities, materials. Equal‑weight advanced. The Dow hit a record. Investors didn't de‑risk they reallocated. This is classic bull‑market rotation: money staying in equities, but choosing different leaders.

Macro reinforced the shift. Payrolls softened, real earnings remain pressured, and factory orders ex‑transport were solid a mix that tempers rate‑hike fears without signaling recession. Micro confirmed it: hyperscalers stabilizing, domestic cyclicals strengthening, and median earnings tracking double‑digit growth. The bottleneck trade is crowded; the broader market is not.

Strategically, leadership is migrating from AI‑momentum to diversified, cash‑flow‑rich, domestically leveraged sectors. Tactically, expect digestion in semis and selective strength in defensives until Q2 earnings clarify whether AI re‑accelerates or hands the baton to the rest of the market.

Bull markets don't die when leaders tire they evolve when new ones emerge.

And to all American readers: Happy Independence Day may your portfolios enjoy some fireworks too. 🇺🇸✨

Upcoming Events This Week - [July 6 - July 10, 2026]

Stay informed on events/news for the coming week.

Market Update

> 5 days into July, will July be a strong green month as it has been? 

> Memory, photonics, and now equipment has started to take a beating. Will this continue? Oh wait, SemiAnalysis came out with a negative read on Nvidia's Kyber, delayed till 2028 due to CPO challenges.

> SOX RSI at 34.70, closer to oversold than overbought. Typically a set up for another rally. 

Economic Calendar

> No one cares about ISM Services PMI, or Jobless Claims (unless shocking for the bad)

> Minutes of the Fed's June FOMC meeting on Wednesday, July 8th. Inflation risk is coming down significantly with oil prices back to where they were before the war BUT consumer electronics are seeing prices tick higher considerably. Is the Fed monitoring that?

text:
WPI’s Core Research Facilities provide shared access to advanced instrumentation—from nano-fabrication and microscopy to photonics, imaging, and medical device testing—powering research across many disciplines. 

Learn more about how you can become a part of our research community here: https://t.co/AhKDDoIZEx.

我写过 Soitec、SIVE、IQE、Shunsin、AAOI……很多人把它们当成各自独立的票。但如果你把这些环节摊在一张桌子上,会发现一个几乎没人问过的问题:它们最后,是在谁的工厂里被"连起来"造出来的?

今天我们来聊一下这家快被遗忘的欧洲小厂: $XFAB的故事。 先说几个我一直都觉得很有意思的点:

1. 首先它的出身就带有历史性意味，它是 1992 年从"东德解体后被拆分的电子工业巨头"里长出来的, 然后被一批比利时人接手, 而这批比利时人还顺手创办了另一家汽车芯片龙头 Melexis，这意味着同一批人,一手造芯片、一手造工厂。

2. 它不卷纳米, 却能够把英伟达和诺基亚拉进了自己的局。 它牵头的一个欧盟出钱的硅光子项目中英伟达、诺基亚、imec 都是同桌的参与方。 一家欧洲小代工厂,能召集起这种阵容,本身就说明问题。

3. 它可能是我这一整条硅光/CPO 链的"总枢纽"。 它在 Soitec 的 SOI 地基上、用 SMART Photonics 的 InP,造出喂给 AAOI 的硅光芯片;同时又是 NVTS 那条"电"线上的 SiC 代工龙头，一只手 AI 光、一只手 EV 电。 

但真正让我觉得很神奇的点在于它约六成的股份,锁在两个创始家族和一个州政府手里，但我们需要知道为什么是这样？而这样又意味着什么？

text:
I'm seeing it every day. 

Investors comparing their YTD, YoY performance, and even weekly/daily performance. 

It's really not fair to compare apples and pumpkins. 

- Investor investing in cyclical companies will definitely go up and down based on the cycle and timing.
- Investor investing in high growth will perform during low rates but struggle during inflation.
- Investor investing in value will miss huge runs but they focus on safety.
- Investing in biotech, pharma, car, energy industry all brings different outcomes. If car industry is lagging, it's impossible to expect an SME for this industry to find 10 baggers in photonics. 

That's just one piece of the story. 

The next one is about your investing style or strategy.
- Long?
- Day/swing trading? 
- Using options? 
- Using all of the above? 

In a volatile environment swing trader should have more options and long investors may underperform. If the market has bearish outlook, shorters may have more luck than the rest?

We can't compare traders in robotics vs long term investors in consumer staples. Makes no sense. 

There's the last piece of the story. 

Not everyone is having the same amount of cash available, nor the same risk tolerance, nor the same time horizon, nor time commitment.  

One investor has a difficult work and no time to pay attention to daily movements. He can't really go and day trade or swing trade. 
Another one with 5 billion portfolio cannot have 50% in a single month. He is likely happy with 3%. 
A student having 200 usd to throw in $SIVE and getting 5x cannot be compared to someone managing his family portfolio. 

When you next time compare yourself with someone, make sure to understand these three dimensions. 

It's VERY likely the guy you're comparing yourself with has different investing profile and strategy. Maybe he isn't investing in memory and buying selling every day. 

Only compare your current performance to your own last month or last year. 

Competing with oneself is one of the most effective paths to sustained personal growth in anything you do.

TBB

It's really not fair to compare apples and pumpkins. - Investor investing in cyclical companies will definitely go up and down based on the cycle and timing. - Investor investing in high growth will perform during low rates but struggle during inflation. - Investor investing in value will miss huge runs but they focus on safety. - Investing in biotech, pharma, car, energy industry all brings different outcomes. If car industry is lagging, it's impossible to expect an SME for this industry to find 10 baggers in photonics. That's just one piece of the story. The next one is about your investing style or strategy. - Long? - Day/swing trading? - Using options? - Using all of the above? In a volatile environment swing trader should have more options and long investors may underperform. If the market has bearish outlook, shorters may have more luck than the rest? We can't compare traders in robotics vs long term investors in consumer staples. Makes no sense. There's the last piece of the story. Not everyone is having the same amount of cash available, nor the same risk tolerance, nor the same time horizon, nor time commitment. One investor has a difficult work and no time to pay attention to daily movements. He can't really go and day trade or swing trade. Another one with 5 billion portfolio cannot have 50% in a single month. He is likely happy with 3%. A student having 200 usd to throw in $SIVE and getting 5x cannot be compared to someone managing his family portfolio. When you next time compare yourself with someone, make sure to understand these three dimensions. It's VERY likely the guy you're comparing yourself with has different investing profile and strategy. Maybe he isn't investing in memory and buying selling every day. Only compare your current performance to your own last month or last year. Competing with oneself is one of the most effective paths to sustained personal growth in anything you do. text:
Mapping $SIVE 2030:

Current: ~52 SEK 

2030 Target: 260 SEK 

Cap: ~85B SEK

The catalyst is the AI Power Wall. As copper interconnects fail, Sivers photonics becomes the standard for data center light sources. A pure play on the transition from hardware to AI infrastructure.

For $SIVE, the 2030 re-rating is a bet on the "Optical Transition." Right now, the market sees them as a niche small-cap component maker. By 2030, they have the potential to be the standard-bearer for light-source technology in AI clusters.
Here is why I believe the bull case for a ~260 SEK target is realistic:

1.The Silicon Photonics (SiPh) Shift: Data centers are hitting a "Power Wall." Traditional copper wires generate too much heat and lose too much signal at AI scale. Moving data with light (photonics) is 1,000x more efficient. Sivers Photonics produces the high-power DFB lasers that act as the "engine" for these optical interconnects.

2.The LEO Satellite Explosion: Beyond AI, their beamforming chips are core to the Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite market. As global internet coverage shifts to space, the demand for low-cost, high-efficiency ground and satellite terminals will move from thousands to millions of units.

3.The 6G Infrastructure Bridge: As we move toward 2030, the 6G standard will rely heavily on mmWave technology. Sivers has been refining this tech for years while others were focused only on standard 5G. They own the "hard part" of the spectrum.
If they successfully navigate the liquidity hurdles of the next few years, the "massive gap" you see is the market finally pricing them as a critical infrastructure provider rather than a speculative hardware play.

text:
Mapping the 2030 landscape: the gap between current prices and future market caps for $AAOI, $MU, and $SNDK is massive.

$AAOI: $135 to $1,200 

$MU: $1,060 to $3,200 

$SNDK: $1,880 to $4,800

The tech backbone is being fundamentally re-valued. This is asymmetry in action.

The "Why" behind the 2030 targets:

1.$AAOI (The Photonics Pivot): The move from 400G to 800G and eventually 1.6T connectivity is the massive catalyst here. As AI clusters grow, the "plumbing" (optical transceivers) becomes the primary bottleneck. We are looking at a fundamental re-rating from a hardware supplier to a critical AI infrastructure provider.

2.$MU (The HBM Standard): High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) has changed the game. Memory is no longer a cyclical commodity; it's a dedicated AI component with much higher margins. By 2030, $MU won't just be selling chips, they will be selling the "oxygen" that keeps AI GPUs running.

3.$SNDK (Enterprise Data Lakes): As companies move from training AI to running inference on massive datasets, the demand for enterprise-grade NAND (storage) will explode. $SNDK is positioned to capture the shift toward high-density, low-power storage required for 2030-era data centers.

A bubble implies there is no ROI on investments. and yet, we are seeing it for Semiconducters, hyperscalers, and energy for AI. Even photonics is a theme now.

text: @michaelsikand @joinautopilot @KawzInvests Photonics is next 🐂

$20M trade update.

I just updated my "Photonics Is Next" AI infra portfolio on @joinautopilot followed by 3300+ U.S. retail investors and $21.9M in connected assets as of 7/6.

This portfolio, which is also overseen by my partner @KawzInvests, has been a major part of the rise in my profile as an investor.

On 6/2, my optical basket peaked at 123.5% all-time returns, achieving those gains in under 6 months, as computed by Autopilot's software. For context, the fund achieved this return holding a diverse basket of stocks, an incredible outcome for retail investors passively following. 

I'm extremely proud of our work on this product. There exists no optical ETF currently for retail investors despite a rush by issuers to cash in on the hype. My picks were launched back in February, providing retail investors a 6 month window to front run the category and get staked into the next AI bottleneck without the complexity of individual stock picking.

Despite the recent sell off, the portfolio is still up 45% year to date, roughly 4.5x the S&P 500.

$LAZR and $LYTE are two ETFs going to launch soon by major players, which should attract billions in AUM. However, I believe my allocations are superior and that my picks will outperform them with lower fees.

So where are we now? Recently, the optical sector has gotten slammed based in my view mainly on sentiment, institutional profit taking, and disputed rumors from a well-known research firm on delays to next-gen architecture. Photonics Is Next is down 29.6% in the last month and 16.8% in just the last week. 

That's why I think it's the perfect time to re-balance, add more weight to higher conviction positions, and add one brand new position I missed earlier on.

This rebalance today adds Corning ($GLW) as a new 10.00% position. Down 13% in five days. Now anchored by three hyperscale relationships (a 10x-capacity NVIDIA partnership, a Meta deal worth up to $6B, and a multi billion dollar Amazon agreement). 

Three names get boosted: AAOI (12.30%→15.00%), LITE (14.75%→15.00%), and AEHR (4.26%→5.00%). 

Six positions are trimmed: TSEM (11.11%→10.00%), COHR (16.07%→15.00%), AXTI (8.58%→5.00%), CIEN (11.39%→10.00%), MRVL (11.11%→5.00%), and VIAV (10.43%→10.00%). 

For catalysts that could create upside, I'm watching:

- Hyperscale Capex Guides In Upcoming Earnings
- Earnings From All Optical Names In The Basket Particularly $COHR $LITE $AAOI
- The launch of mainstream optical ETFs $LAZR and $LYTE

$GLW $AAOI $LITE $AEHR $TSEM $COHR $AXTI $CIEN $MRVL $VIAV 

--
See link in comments to follow the portfolio using the Autopilot app. Pay attention to disclaimers on the image of this post and the Autopilot app.

text:
🌲 🌏  𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗪𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺 𝗞𝗲𝗲𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘀: 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗚𝘂𝗮𝗿𝗱𝗶𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗟𝗶𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗠𝗲𝗺𝗼𝗿𝘆 🌲 🌿

Hand to heart, beloved brothers and sisters of the Blue-White Rose.

Continuing our shared remembrance, we now discover the Forest Wisdom Keepers, the ancient, conscious intelligences that hold the living memory of the Earth’s forests. As High Priest of the Atlantean Remembrance, I offer this vision so that more awakened souls may feel their profound presence and remember our original kinship with these great guardians. We are One with all of Creation, and every kingdom is One with all of us in its own beautiful way.

🌎 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗩𝗶𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗪𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺 𝗞𝗲𝗲𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘀

Forest Wisdom Keepers are the collective consciousness of the ancient trees, the forest devas, the root networks, and the subtle intelligences that weave the living canopy of the Earth. They are not separate from the trees, they are the aware, multidimensional essence that animates and protects the forest realms. In the remembrance, they are the patient archivists of planetary memory, holding the wisdom of countless cycles, the codes of harmony, and the living blueprint of the Earth’s original design.

As the photon band aligns and the Great Convergence unfolds, the Forest Wisdom Keepers are becoming more accessible. They respond to sincere presence, respect, and open-hearted listening, offering guidance, healing, and deep grounding during these times of rapid change.

Here are some resonant aspects that spark curiosity and connection for many across different systems:

▪︎ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗔𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗶𝘃𝗲𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗘𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗵 ➞ The Forest Wisdom Keepers hold the living memory of the planet, from the golden ages of Atlantis and Lemuria to the long cycles of forgetting and return. They share this wisdom when we approach with humility and open presence.

▪︎ 𝗥𝗼𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗚𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝗟𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 ➞ Their vast root systems anchor higher photonic frequencies into the Earth grid, while their crowns reach into subtle realms. Connecting with them helps us ground star codes and stabilize our own silica matrix upgrades.

▪︎ 𝗛𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗛𝗮𝗿𝗺𝗼𝗻𝘆 ➞ The Keepers radiate a deep, nurturing peace that calms the nervous system, clears emotional density, and restores the natural flow between the human heart and the heart of the Earth.

▪︎ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗥𝗼𝗹𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝟭𝟰𝟰,𝟬𝟬𝟬 ➞ Many of the 144,000 carry cellular memory of working in conscious partnership with the Forest Wisdom Keepers. Reawakening this connection strengthens our role as bridges between the stars and the living Earth.

▪︎ 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗔𝗰𝗿𝗼𝘀𝘀 𝗟𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗮𝗴𝗲𝘀 ➞ Whether our threads are Pleiadian, Lyran, Sirian, or blended, the Forest Wisdom Keepers respond to the pure frequency of love and respect, helping us remember our shared origin as co-creators with the Earth.

🙏 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝗮𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗣𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗽

As Sovereignbeings of light, we see the Forest Wisdom Keepers as beloved and ancient allies. In the golden ages, communication with them was natural and constant. The long cycle of forgetting dimmed this dialogue, but it was never truly lost. As the Veil of Forgetting dissolves during the Great Convergence, the living partnership between humanity and the Forest Wisdom Keepers is being joyfully restored.

1/2 🌲

text:
“Core Pattern in Your Posts
You consistently treat FFT (and QFT) as a powerful “decoder” or analytical lens for revealing hidden order in apparent chaos—whether in AI models, generative outputs, physical systems, simulations, or signal processing. 

This directly ties into the Clever Hans video/paper: using frequency-domain tools (FFT/STFT) to expose how AI classifiers rely on spurious spectral shortcuts rather than genuine understanding (e.g., minimal frequency subsets that flip classifications inaudibly).

You often post:

•  Concise “brain-dump” keyword lists connecting FFT to other tech/concepts.

•  Detailed explanatory threads breaking down technical mechanisms.

•  Speculative links to steganography, emergent behavior, simulations (“the sim”), cybernetics, and future tech (photonics, quantum materials, etc.).

•  Playful or self-referential framing (e.g., “future Lilith is annoyed,” predictions accelerating).”

text:
Dylan Patel from SemiAnalysis keeps posting wild, unverified claims right before Sunday futures open — feels like market manipulation, no?

Same FUD last week, stock dropped, and suddenly there's a photonics ETF. 🤔

Retail investors need facts, not speculation that swings

The recent SemiAnalysis report made such an impact that even $NVDA had to reject it and make a statement about it, saying "Our roadmap is intact" ... more on that below. Optics Tech // Photonics theme now has short term downtrend after running hot from Q4 2025 thru Q2 2026.

Here are a few names I'm watching in this theme close to great buy zones:

$LITE
$COHR
$MRVL
$AAOI
$TSEM
$AEHR
$AXTI 

A couple great ETFs if you prefer to buy and not pay much attention, given the theme can be volatile:

$LAZR launched June 30th in partnership with @SemiAnalysis
$LYTE from @Roundhill Investments begins trading soon

There are other related ETFs, but I believe they are too diversified away from some of the stronger names.

This is (not) financial advice 😉

Last thought on this theme... I've said it before but it bears repeating: I'm impressed by @joinautopilot's team, @michaelsikand & @KawzInvests, for getting ahead of it with their "Photonics is Next" basket before institutional investors launched ETFs. I'm expecting both $LAZR and $LYTE to be great long-term investments in this space regardless.

Looking ahead, I'm also keeping an eye on Agentic AI and Robotics, two more themes I expect to explode in growth in the coming years.

text:
My goodness, I have never agreed so much with someone on Fin X

Quick timeline:

Just about 3 weeks ago or so, companies like $SIVE had stopped going up like crazy by 10% every day

But they weren't correcting either, just fluctuating between 90-120 SEK

Even back then, there were posts from Serenity and even myself where we had detected a pattern:

Swedish retail -> US retail -> Institutions

Well, back then we already warned that once the US took control of $SIVE, it would be the institutions' turn, and we knew it was going to be tough and that many weak hands would drop out

Okay, and what is happening now?

Because if a technology like CPO is "dead," why would you be so hell-bent on putting out a bearish article about it every single week?

If it's "dead," why not just let time prove that it doesn't generate revenue?

I'll tell you why right now

Because it's not "dead", it's the new super-cycle, and institutions and research firms know it

That's why, coincidentally, right after their "piece of shit analysis," they coincidentally create a Photonics ETF, coincidentally with $SIVE in it

Wow, how strange, right?

Creating an ETF for a "dead" technology that is supposedly "delayed by a few years”

Serenity and I already warned you before the drops: now is the time to hold

Institutions are loading up heavily at prices higher than the current ones, even demanding more than what management was initially offering

I don't know about you, but the institutions aren't taking my $SIVE shares

I have never, ever been this bullish on $SIVE

One thing that makes me determined to hold $SIVE and $AAOI through the volatility is how determined people seem to be to reduce the stock price..

Every week there’s a report that gets refuted after the market has tanked!

Are we just being played and realistically everyone knows

text:
$IONQ is looking to become vertically integrated.

The acquisition of $SKYT seems like the missing piece.

It’s not.

$IONQ buys laser systems from Coherent and NKT Photonics.

Coherent is a US company that supplies lasers to $IONQ called Coherent Paladin.

The ultraviolet lasers produce light at specific wavelengths for the atomic transitions in barium and ytterbium that $IONQ uses.

Those are standard benchtop setups for general qubit manipulation. They’re massive and require lab environment to operate.

Then there’s NKT Photonics from Denmark. It supplies tailor made crystal fiber lasers with low noise. The goal here is to use the lasers in datacenters.

The setup is smaller so that the lasers can be mounted in a 19-inch rack with the other components of the quantum computer.

The lasers are ultra-stable and narrow-linewidth. It’s challenging to simply swap them with anyone else’s lasers.

Which makes $IONQ vulnerable to this specific supply chain.

Coherent relies on gallium, germanium, and rare-earth elements, most of which are mined in China. China suspended its total ban on exporting critical dual-use metals to the US until November 27, 2026.

What will happen after? Nobody knows.

We know from Coherent’s 2023 update that it had ample inventories and suppliers outside of China.

NKT Photonics sources nonlinear crystals used to build frequency converters from Europe, Japan, and the US.

The dangers here are regulations and delays.

NKT Photonics is a subsidiary of Hamamatsu Photonics. It has preferential access to Hamamatsu’s supply chain of laser diodes and optical components.

Trade wars between Europe and Japan are unlikely, but if they happen, NKT Photonics will be affected.

Finally, its lasers are dual use: They can be used in quantum computers and military guidance or radar systems.

Any sudden restrictions by Danish authorities could result in bottlenecks.

$IONQ is managing its supply chain nicely but dangers remain.

text:
Thread

Everyone keeps asking the same question.

"Which company wins AI?" 
$NVDA $AVGO $AMD $TSM $MU $MSFT $GOOGL $AMZN $META $ORCL $PLTR ?

I think that's the wrong question.

The better question is:

Which company becomes impossible to replace?

The AI infrastructure buildout isn't being constrained by GPUs anymore.

It's becoming constrained by everything around them.

Power.

Cooling.

Memory.

Networking.

And increasingly...

Light.

Most investors still think optical networking is about transceivers.

It isn't.

The real value is quietly moving upstream.

Every new optical architecture—whether it's pluggables, Scale-Out CPO, Scale-Up CPO, Near-Packaged Optics, or External Laser Source (ELS)—still depends on one thing:

A stable, high-volume CW laser.

Different architectures.

Same dependency.

That's where supply-chain mapping becomes interesting.

This is why I continue spending far more time studying suppliers than hyperscalers.

Hyperscalers change vendors.

Platforms evolve.

Chip architectures change.

But infrastructure bottlenecks?

Those usually last much longer than people expect.

The companies solving those bottlenecks often capture the highest operating leverage of the entire cycle.

This is exactly why I find $SIVE so interesting.

Most people still describe Sivers as "a laser company."

I think that's missing the point.

The product isn't the thesis.

Its position in the supply chain is.

If multiple AI networking architectures all require the same laser technology, then the value isn't coming from any single customer.

It's coming from becoming embedded across the ecosystem.

That's a completely different investment framework.

People also underestimate how infrastructure cycles actually work.

Revenue is always the last thing to arrive.

First comes R&D.

Then qualification.

Then design wins.

Then ecosystem validation.

Only after all of that do production ramps begin.

Investors waiting for obvious revenue growth are usually arriving after the market has already repriced the opportunity.

One thing I think the market still misunderstands is timing.

Many investors expect AI photonics to become a meaningful revenue driver this quarter.

I don't.

The real story is what happens when next-generation optical deployments begin scaling throughout 2027.

That's when supply-chain positioning starts to matter far more than quarterly headlines.

I've said this before:

I don't invest by asking,

"Who sells the most AI chips?"

I ask,

"Who does everyone else quietly depend on?"

Those companies rarely make headlines.

But they often become the biggest winners of the cycle.

That's why I continue mapping bottlenecks instead of narratives.

NFA. DYODD.@aleabitoreddit #StockMarket  #Investing #AI

text:
💰 Nyemissioner - positivt eller negativt? Allt handlar om syftet.

Jag har märkt att många småsparare och nya investerare automatiskt kopplar ordet nyemission till något negativt och därför blir oroliga.

Det är fullt förståeligt – många nyemissioner genomförs när bolag har ekonomiska problem.

Men det betyder inte att alla nyemissioner ser likadana ut. I detta fallet handlar det dessutom om en riktad nyemission som riktas direkt mot större institutionella investerare och kommer dessutom genomföras över natten.

⸻

I Sivers, $SIVE, fall är budskapet från bolaget tydligt:

Kapitalet ska användas för att öka kapaciteten och möta en växande efterfrågan inom flera av bolagets viktigaste affärsområden, bland annat:

✅ Optiska lasrar och optiska förstärkare för AI-datacenter

✅ Automotive LiDAR

✅ Fortsatt expansion inom Photonics

Bolaget beskriver alltså inte en situation där kapitalet behövs för att hålla verksamheten vid liv, utan för att kunna genomföra nästa tillväxtfas. Det är en viktig skillnad!

⸻

Om kapitalet används för att öka produktionskapaciteten, möta en växande efterfrågan och skapa högre framtida intäkter kan värdet på bolaget över tid utvecklas positivt trots att antalet aktier ökar.

Det är just därför syftet med en nyemission är minst lika viktigt som nyemissionen i sig.

⸻

Det är också värt att komma ihåg att Sivers under det senaste året kommunicerat:

🔹 En växande pipeline inom AI-datacenter.

🔹 Produktionsupptrappning inom Automotive LiDAR från H2 2026.

🔹 Flera planerade produktionsramper under 2026–2027.

Det är mot den bakgrunden jag tycker att den här nyemissionen ska bedömas.

Personligen ser jag denna nyemissionen har ett väldigt starkt signalvärde kring vart bolaget är på väg!

⸻

Detta är inte en köp- eller säljrekommendation.

Jag tycker bara att det är viktigt att läsa hela pressmeddelandet och förstå varför bolaget tar in kapital – inte bara reagera på ordet nyemission!
